title: Warnings: Violation symptoms indicating architecture erosion
abstract: As a software system evolves, its architecture tends to degrade, and gradually impedes software maintenance and evolution activities and negatively impacts the quality attributes of the system. The main root cause behind architecture erosion phenomenon derives from violation symptoms (i.e., various architecturally-relevant violations, such as violations of architecture pattern). Previous studies focus on detecting violations in software systems using architecture conformance checking approaches. However, code review comments are also rich sources that may contain extensive discussions regarding architecture violations, while there is a limited understanding of violation symptoms from the viewpoint of developers
venue: Information and Software Technology
ta_rationale: Título sugere foco em arquitetura de software e erosão (tema relevante a processos de SW), mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se aplica process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de métricas de processo conforme escopo PATHCAST. Pode ser estudo sobre detecção de problemas arquiteturais sem relação com event logs ou predição de processos.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda detecção de violações arquiteturais e erosão de arquitetura em sistemas de software através de análise de code review comments e conformance checking, mas não aplica process mining (descoberta de processos, event log analysis, workflow mining), modelagem estocástica (Markov, Monte Carlo, Petri nets estocásticas) nem forecasting de métricas de processo de desenvolvimento (lead time, cycle time, throughput). Trata-se de um estudo de qualidade de código/arquitetura, não de mineração

title: Agile Planning and Tracking of Software Projects Using the State-Space Approach
venue: Communications in Computer and Information Science
ta_rationale: O título sugere forte relevância para IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW via state-space approach) e potencialmente IC3 (tracking de projetos ágeis). Porém, sem abstract não é possível confirmar se aborda realmente process mining, event logs ou forecasting de métricas de processo, ou se é apenas um método de gestão de projetos genérico.
ft_rationale: O paper atende IC2 e IC3: propõe uma técnica de rastreamento de projetos ágeis usando state-space approach com simulação Monte Carlo, que é modelagem estocástica aplicada a processos de desenvolvimento de software; inclui avaliação de tracking e previsão de status de projeto através de simulação. O texto completo confirma a aplicação a software projects com método estocástico explícito.

title: Distributed process discovery and conformance checking
abstract: Process mining techniques have matured over the last decade and more and more organization started to use this new technology. The two most important types of process mining are process discovery (i.e., learning a process model from example behavior recorded in an event log) and conformance checking (i.e., comparing modeled behavior with observed behavior). Process mining is motivated by the availability of event data. However, as event logs become larger (say terabytes), performance becomes a concern. The only way to handle larger applications while ensuring acceptable response times, is to distribute analysis over a network of computers (e.g., multicore systems, grids, and clouds). This paper provides an overview of the different ways in which process mining problems can be distributed. We identify three types of distribution: replication, a horizontal partitioning of the event log, and a vertical partitioning of the event log. These types are discussed in the context of both procedural (e.g., Petri nets) and declarative process models. Most challenging is the horizontal partitioning of event logs in the context of procedural models. Therefore, a new approach to decompose Petri nets and associated event logs is presented. This approach illustrates that process mining problems can be distributed in various ways
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: Título sugere relevância a process mining (descoberta e conformance checking), potencialmente atendendo IC1, mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação clara de aplicação a processos de software. Pode ser trabalho genérico de BPM sem foco em SW.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de técnicas de process mining (descoberta e conformance checking) em contexto genérico de escalabilidade distribuída, mas não demonstra aplicação específica a processos de software (SW). O resumo aborda decomposição de Petri nets e particionamento de event logs em escala, sem evidência de avaliação em artefatos de SW (commits, issues, CI/CD) ou desenvolvimento de software. Caracteriza-se como trabalho de BPM/process mining teórico-técnico, não vinculado ao domínio de engenharia de

title: Extracting Process Features from Event Logs to Learn Coarse-Grained Simulation Models
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: O título sugere forte relevância para IC1 e IC2 (extração de features de event logs para modelos de simulação estocásticos), mas a ausência de resumo impede confirmação do domínio de aplicação (se é SW ou outro domínio) e avaliação clara dos critérios de exclusão.
ft_rationale: O paper apresenta uma abordagem clara de process mining (extração de features de event logs) combinada com simulação estocástica (System Dynamics) para criar modelos coarse-grained de processos. Atende IC1 (process mining aplicado a event logs) e IC2 (modelagem estocástica via System Dynamics para processos). Não há evidência de EC decisivo — o domínio de aplicação não é explicitamente não-SW, e a técnica é aplicada a processos de negócio genéricos que abrangem SW.

title: Representing software usage models with stochastic automata networks
abstract: The benefits of usage models in the statistical testing of software have been recognized by the software engineering community and discrete state formalisms have been largely used to describe such usage models of software. The Markov Chains formalism (MC) is a natural choice to do so. However, in this paper we suggest the use of a more sophisticated discrete state formalism, the Stochastic Automata Networks (SAN). In many problems of the performance evaluation area, the SAN models present advantages over MC models. Therefore, it seems natural to us to verify similar advantages in the modeling of usage models.This paper presents a case study of building a software usage model using SAN formalism. A software tool called DOCSEDITOR is modeled using MC and SAN. The models are compared in terms of number of states, scalability, and readability. It is not the objective of this paper to present a full framework to develop and analyse usage models with SAN, but just to show some evident advantages of the use of SAN instead of MC. In order to do this, the conclusion points out the indexes than can be computed from both models and suggests the next steps on future work.
venue: ACM International Conference Proceeding Series
ta_rationale: O título sugere uso de stochastic automata networks (modelagem estocástica) em contexto de software, alinhado potencialmente com IC2. Porém, sem abstract disponível, não é possível confirmar se o 'software usage models' refere-se a processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC2) ou a modelagem de comportamento de usuários/sistemas (fora do escopo de processos de SW). A falta de abstrato impede avaliação conclusiva.
ft_rationale: O paper apresenta modelagem estocástica (SAN e MC) aplicada a 'software usage models' — modelos de comportamento de usuários/sistema — não a processos de desenvolvimento de software. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, CI/CD), discovery de fluxos de desenvolvimento, ou forecasting de métricas de processo de engenharia. Enquadra-se em EC1 (domínio fora de SW — simulação de uso de sistema, não processo de desenvolvimento) e EC2 (software como ferramenta/objeto de mode

title: Unsupervised discovery of intentional process models from event logs
abstract: Research on guidance and method engineering has highlighted that many method engineering issues, such as lack of flexibility or adaptation, are solved more effectively when intentions are explicitly specified. However, software engineering process models are most often described in terms of sequences of activities. This paper presents a novel approach, so-called Map Miner Method (MMM), designed to automate the construction of intentional process models from process logs. To do so, MMM uses Hidden Markov Models to model users' activities logs in terms of users' strategies. MMM also infers users' intentions and constructs fine-grained and coarse-grained intentional process models with respect to the Map metamodel syntax (i.e., metamodel that specifies intentions and strategies of process actors). These models are obtained by optimizing a new precision-fitness metric. The result is a software engineering method process specification aligned with state of the art of method engineering approaches. As a case study, the MMM is used to mine the intentional process associated to the Eclipse platform usage. Observations show that the obtained intentional process model offers a new understanding of software processes, and could readily be used for recommender systems.
venue: 11th Working Conference on Mining Software Repositories MSR 2014 Proceedings
ta_rationale: Título altamente relevante (découverte de modelos de processo em event logs) e venue de excelência (MSR 2014) indicam forte potencial para IC1 (process mining em SW). Porém, ausência de resumo impede confirmação definitiva do foco em processos de SW versus análise genérica de BPM.
ft_rationale: O paper apresenta Map Miner Method (MMM), uma abordagem que usa Hidden Markov Models para minerar event logs de atividades de usuários em processos de software (caso de estudo: uso da plataforma Eclipse). Atende claramente IC1 (process mining em SW) ao aplicar técnicas de descoberta de modelos de processo a artefatos de software. Não há EC aplicável: o domínio é claramente engenharia de software, a modelagem é orientada ao processo (não apenas ferramenta), e a avaliação ocorre em contexto real d

title: Using Model-Based Systems Engineering Tools in Software Development
abstract: The article discusses the application of model-based systems engineering in software development. MBSE offers a new model-based approach to viewing complex systems, allowing data and information to be effectively shared across different product life cycle stages. The paper examines the model-based systems engineering methodology, its benefits, and its impact on requirements, design, analysis, and verification within the product life cycle. The study highlights the relevance of using model-based systems engineering to modern systems engineering practices, especially in the context of software engineering. The study results demonstrate the benefits of model-based systems engineering and its ability to optimize development processes, improve product quality, and reduce delivery times. The scientific novelty of the article lies in the application and research of the model-based systems engineering in the context of software development, which represents a significant contribution to the development of modern systems engineering practices and development technologies.
venue: Procedia Computer Science
ta_rationale: Título sugere ferramentas MBSE em desenvolvimento de software, potencialmente relevante para modelagem de processos, mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting ou mineração de repositórios conforme escopo PATHCAST. Risco de ser apenas sobre ferramentas de engenharia de software (EC2) sem foco em análise/predição de processos.
ft_rationale: O resumo descreve a aplicação de MBSE (Model-Based Systems Engineering) como abordagem metodológica para desenvolvimento de software, focando em benefícios gerais (qualidade, tempo de entrega, compartilhamento de dados). Não há evidência de process mining (descoberta/análise de event logs), modelagem estocástica (Markov, Monte Carlo, Petri nets), forecasting de métricas de processo (lead time, cycle time), ou mineração de repositórios. O paper trata MBSE como ferramenta/metodologia de engenharia

title: A review on software process mining using petri nets
venue: Asian Journal of Applied Sciences
ta_rationale: O título sugere forte alinhamento com IC1 (Process Mining em Software usando Petri nets) e IC2 (Modelagem Estocástica com Petri nets em processos de SW). Porém, a ausência de resumo impede confirmação de que o escopo é efetivamente SW e não BPM genérico, além de não permitir verificar se há componentes de forecasting (IC3) ou mineração de repositórios (IC4).
ft_rationale: O texto completo confirma IC1 (Process Mining em Software com Petri Nets) e IC2 (Modelagem Estocástica — Petri Nets — em processos de SW). O paper é uma revisão que aborda explicitamente mining de processos de software usando Petri Nets, síntese de modelos a partir de logs de sistemas de gerenciamento de documentos e versão, alinhado diretamente ao escopo PATHCAST. Nenhum EC decisivo se aplica.

title: A survey on workflow verifications of petri nets based service process management
abstract: Service process management is a cross-discipline research topic, including computer science, management technology and economics. How to effectively integrate service resources to meet customer demands becomes a key problem to service-oriented software development. Most of existing works are in the early research stage. The scope of process management consists of process design, process execution, process mining, and process intelligence. However, it requires process modeling, which is a crucial precondition in the lifecycle of service process management. In this paper, it reviews workflow verifications of Petri Nets based service process management. First, the workflow and description supporting tools are introduced. Then, considering the composite service is an effective way to organize third-party business functions seamlessly, the Petri Nets based service workflow employed to formalizing composition behaviors is discussed. Third, components encapsulated in the composite service may be encountered functional and non-functional problems since services are offered by different organizations. The functional verification includes the correctness, reachability and safety. The non-functional verification is to evaluate the performance of Petri Nets based workflow model ranging over timed constraints and probabilistic behaviors. Therefore, it summarizes Petri Nets based workflow verification techniques to guarantee the quality of services composition. In conclusion, this survey provides academic references to researchers and developers in service computing and process management.
venue: International Journal of Grid and Distributed Computing
ta_rationale: Título indica foco em verificação de workflows com Petri nets em 'service process management', potencialmente relevante para IC2 (modelagem estocástica). Contudo, sem abstract disponível, é impossível confirmar: (a) se refere-se especificamente a processos de software ou domínios genéricos (BPM, manufatura, serviços); (b) se aplica Petri nets estocásticas ou apenas síntese/verificação clássica; (c) se há conexão com PM, forecasting ou repositórios de SW.
ft_rationale: O survey trata verificação de workflows com Petri nets em 'service process management' genérico (composição de serviços, BPM), não em processos de desenvolvimento de software específicos. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD), nem de forecasting de métricas de processo ou mineração de repositórios. Aplica-se EC1 (domínio fora de SW — serviços e BPM genéricos) e nenhum IC é claramente atendido no contexto de engenharia de software.

title: An integrated infrastructure using process mining techniques for software process verification
abstract: The verification of process implementations according to specifications is a critical step of process management. This verification must be practiced according to objective criteria and evidence. This study explains an integrated infrastructure that utilizes process mining for software process verification and case studies carried out by using this infrastructure. Specific software providing the utilization of process mining algorithms for software process verification is developed as a plugin to an open-source EPF Composer tool that supports the management of software and system engineering processes. With three case studies, bug management, task management, and defect management processes are verified against defined and established process models (modeled by using EPF Composer) by using this plugin over real process data. Among these, the results of the case study performed in a large, leading IT solutions company in Turkey are remarkable in demonstrating the opportunities for process improvement.
venue: Computer Systems and Software Engineering Concepts Methodologies Tools and Applications
ta_rationale: Título fortemente indicativo de IC1 (process mining para verificação de processos de software), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo, metodologia e se realmente aplica PM a artefatos de SW ou apenas a modelos genéricos de processo.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente IC1: aplica process mining (algoritmos de PM) para verificação de processos de software, usando dados reais de processos (bug management, task management, defect management) contra modelos de processo definidos. Desenvolveu plugin para EPF Composer que utiliza PM sobre event logs reais de processos de SW. Nenhum EC decisivo identificado.

title: An integrated infrastructure using process mining techniques for software process verification
abstract: The verification of process implementations according to specifications is a critical step of process management. This verification must be practiced according to objective criteria and evidence. This study explains an integrated infrastructure that utilizes process mining for software process verification and case studies carried out by using this infrastructure. Specific software providing the utilization of process mining algorithms for software process verification is developed as a plugin to an open-source EPF Composer tool that supports the management of software and system engineering processes. With three case studies, bug management, task management, and defect management processes are verified against defined and established process models (modeled by using EPF Composer) by using this plugin over real process data. Among these, the results of the case study performed in a large, leading IT solutions company in Turkey are remarkable in demonstrating the opportunities for process improvement.
venue: Research Methods Concepts Methodologies Tools and Applications
ta_rationale: Título fortemente sugestivo de process mining aplicado a verificação de processos de software (IC1), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo, domínio e se realmente trata de SW development processes versus BPM genérico.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica explicitamente process mining (conformance checking) a artefatos e processos de software (bug management, task management, defect management processes), desenvolvendo uma infraestrutura e plugin específicos para verificação de processos de desenvolvimento de SW contra modelos definidos usando dados reais. Atende claramente IC1 sem EC decisivo.

title: Application of Markov chain approach for multi-attributes dynamic software reliability assessment under both AHP and gray correlation methods
abstract: Measuring software trustworthiness is an important field in trusted software assessment. In this paper, a dynamic model of multi-attributes dynamic software reliability assessment is proposed based on analytic hierarchy process (AHP) and gray correlation, since current software assessing methods cannot fully meet the accuracy and objectivity of the assessment. Our novel model of software trustworthiness comprehensive evaluation index system is established, where AHP comprehensive evaluation method is introduced to determine the trustworthy indicators and the weight of the sub-attributes. Meanwhile, group expert judgments are used to modify group decision results and the Euclid distance is used to judge the value’s change of state checkpoints. In addition, the software properties probabilistic transformation model of each level of sub-properties is constructed to accomplish dynamic software reliability assessment using the methods of Markov chain process and gray correlation model. Finally, an example is used to illustrate the effectiveness of our dynamic software reliability assessment.
venue: International Journal of Modern Physics C
ta_rationale: Título sugere aplicação de Markov chain em confiabilidade de software (potencial IC2), mas sem abstract é impossível confirmar se aborda modelagem estocástica de processos de SW (desenvolvimento) ou apenas confiabilidade/qualidade de produtos. A ausência de resumo impede avaliação definitiva do escopo.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica Markov chain e modelagem estocástica (IC2 potencial), mas exclusivamente para avaliação estática de confiabilidade e trustworthiness de software como produto, não para modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento de SW. Não há evidence de aplicação a artefatos de processo (commits, issues, event logs) ou descoberta de padrões de processo de desenvolvimento. O foco é qualidade/confiabilidade do produto final, não engenharia de processos de SW.
ft_matched_ec: EC1 - O domínio é avaliação de confiabilidade de software como produto, não processos de desenvolvimento de software|EC3 - Método de avaliação teórico sem aplicação a processos de SW (development workflows, CI/CD, issue tracking, etc.)

title: Constructing hybrid software process simulation models
authors: Chao Gao; He Zhang; Shu Jiang
abstract: Software process simulation (SPS) has become an active research area for managing and improving software development processes since its introduction in the last two decades. Hybrid simulation, the combination of simulation paradigms to address a problem, is becoming more popular as the problems we are presented with become more complex. However, integrating multiple simulation paradigms faces the issues of compatibility, interoperatability and synchronization when executing simulation. The objective of this research is to present the state-of-the-art of this research area, the hybrid mechanism when integrating paradigms, and more importantly provide practical support for the effective adoption of hybrid simulation in SPS context. Based on an extended systematic literature review, this paper presents the preliminary results by answering the research questions. Depending upon the way these simulation paradigms represent different aspects and levels of software process and the context in which they can be modeled by SPS, two hybrid mechanisms: Hierarchical Mechanism and Interlinked Mechanism, have been frequently employed. The detailed discussions of integration strategies and recommendations when applying hybrid simulation may offer reference value to the SPS community.
venue: Proceedings of the 2015 International Conference on Software and System Process
ta_rationale: Título sugere forte relevância para IC2 (modelagem estocástica de processos de SW) e potencialmente IC1, mas ausência de resumo impede avaliação definitiva de escopo, metodologia e aplicação específica a processos de desenvolvimento de software.
ft_rationale: O paper é uma revisão sistemática (SLR) sobre hybrid simulation em software process simulation, focando em mecanismos de integração de paradigmas de simulação. Embora aborde modelagem de processos de SW, não apresenta aplicação concreta de process mining, modelagem estocástica específica (Markov, Monte Carlo, Petri nets), forecasting de métricas ou mineração de repositórios. É uma revisão secundária sobre SPS que não trata especificamente de PM ou estocástico em SE conforme IC2-IC4 requerem. Cai

title: Coverage-Based Greybox Fuzzing as Markov Chain
abstract: Coverage-based Greybox Fuzzing (CGF) is a random testing approach that requires no program analysis. A new test is generated by slightly mutating a seed input. If the test exercises a new and interesting path, it is added to the set of seeds; otherwise, it is discarded. We observe that most tests exercise the same few “high-frequency” paths and develop strategies to explore significantly more paths with the same number of tests by gravitating towards low-frequency paths. We explain the challenges and opportunities of CGF using a Markov chain model which specifies the probability that fuzzing the seed that exercises path i generates an input that exercises path j. Each state (i.e., seed) has an energy that specifies the number of inputs to be generated from that seed. We show that CGF is considerably more efficient if energy is inversely proportional to the density of the stationary distribution and increases monotonically every time that seed is chosen. Energy is controlled with a power schedule. We implemented several schedules by extending AFL. In 24 hours, AFLFast exposes 3 previously unreported CVEs that are not exposed by AFL and exposes 6 previously unreported CVEs 7x faster than AFL. AFLFast produces at least an order of magnitude more unique crashes than AFL. We compared AFLFast to the symbolic executor Klee. In terms of vulnerability detection, AFLFast is significantly more effective than Klee on the same subject programs that were discussed in the original Klee paper. In terms of code coverage, AFLFast only slightly outperforms Klee while a combination of both tools achieves best results by mitigating the individual weaknesses.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (Markov Chain) aplicada a fuzzing, que é técnica de teste de SW, não processo de desenvolvimento. Sem abstract, é incerto se o paper trata de processos de SW (IC2/IC4) ou apenas de algoritmo de teste (EC3). Necessária leitura completa para classificação definitiva.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica modelagem estocástica (Markov Chain) a fuzzing, que é uma técnica de teste de software, não a processos de desenvolvimento de software. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de processo (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD), descoberta de processo, ou forecasting de métricas de processo. Enquadra-se em EC3 — algoritmo teórico (power schedule para fuzzing) sem avaliação em processos de SW.

title: Examining the attributes of transitions between team roles in the software development projects
abstract: In project management, efficiency of team performance is often associated with the balance in terms of its team roles. All explanations, however, as well as conclusions that relate to balancing a project team, were obtained verbally and are not confirmed either theoretically or empirically. Therefore, it is appropriate to examine the transitions between team roles based on mathematical modeling. To understand regularities in the transitions of team members between team roles, we constructed a transition model based on the discrete Markov chain and defined parameters of the model. Using the simulation, we performed the study and demonstrated that it is natural for a software development project team to balance the team roles. This attribute does not depend on the initial composition of the team in terms of team roles. A difference in initial conditions only affects the rate of change in the probabilities of individual team roles, but the attribute of reducing the difference between the values of probabilities of individual team roles persists. The results obtained confirm that by helping some team members to take on secondary roles to prevent the duplication of roles or to resolve certain problems, a project manager may influence the balancing of team in terms of team roles.
venue: Eastern European Journal of Enterprise Technologies
ta_rationale: O título sugere análise de transições entre papéis em projetos de desenvolvimento de software, o que pode estar relacionado a process mining ou modelagem de processos de SW. Porém, sem acesso ao resumo, não é possível confirmar se o trabalho efetivamente aplica process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting em artefatos/logs de SW, ou se trata apenas de análise qualitativa de estruturas organizacionais.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica modelagem de Markov (IC2 parcialmente satisfeito) para estudar transições entre papéis de equipe em projetos de SW. Contudo, não há evidência de (1) mineração de event logs ou repositórios reais (sem IC1/IC4), (2) forecasting/predição de métricas de processo (sem IC3), ou (3) validação empírica em artefatos de SW (commits, issues, logs). O estudo é puramente teórico/simulado, analisando estrutura organizacional abstrata sem dados de processos de desenvolvimento reais. Assim, nenhu

title: Genetic-based anomaly detection in logs of process aware systems
abstract: Nowaday-s, many organizations use systems that support business process as a whole or partially. However, in some application domains, like software development and health care processes, a normative Process Aware System (PAS) is not suitable, because a flexible support is needed to respond rapidly to new process models. On the other hand, a flexible Process Aware System may be vulnerable to undesirable and fraudulent executions, which imposes a tradeoff between flexibility and security. In order to make this tradeoff available, a genetic-based anomaly detection model for logs of Process Aware Systems is presented in this paper. The detection of an anomalous trace is based on discovering an appropriate process model by using genetic process mining and detecting traces that do not fit the appropriate model as anomalous trace; therefore, when used in PAS, this model is an automated solution that can support coexistence of flexibility and security.
venue: World Academy of Science Engineering and Technology
ta_rationale: Título sugere process mining (anomaly detection em logs de sistemas process-aware), potencialmente relevante para IC1, mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se aplica-se especificamente a processos de software ou a domínios genéricos de BPM. Necessária avaliação de texto completo.
ft_rationale: O resumo menciona process mining (descoberta de modelo e detecção de anomalias em logs) e PAS, mas não demonstra aplicação específica a processos de desenvolvimento de software. Embora cite 'software development' como exemplo de domínio que usa PAS, o trabalho é genérico para Process Aware Systems em geral (também menciona healthcare). Não há evidência de mineração de artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou forecasting de métricas de processo de SW — trata apenas detecção de anomalias em

title: Mapping activity diagram to Petri Net: Application of Markov theory for analyzing non-functional parameters
abstract: The quality of an architectural design of a software system has a great influence on achieving non-functional requirements of a system. A regular software development project is often influenced by non-functional factors such as the customers' expectations about the performance and reliability of the software as well as the reduction of underlying risks. The evaluation of non- functional parameters of a software system at the early stages of design and its development process are often considered as major factors in dealing with these issues. Because these evaluations can help us to choose the most proper model which is the securest and the most reliable.In this paper, a method is presented to obtain performance parameters from Generalized Stochastic Petri Net (GSPN) to be able to analyze the stochastic behavior of the system. The embedded Continuous Time Markov Chain (CTMC) is derived from the GSPN and the Markov chain theory is used to obtain the performance parameters.
venue: International Journal of Engineering Transactions B Applications
ta_rationale: O título indica mapeamento de activity diagrams para Petri nets com teoria de Markov, sugerindo modelagem estocástica (IC2). Porém, sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se a aplicação é em processos de software ou em domínios genéricos (EC1). A ausência de abstract impede avaliação conclusiva do escopo.
ft_rationale: O artigo trata de modelagem estocástica (GSPN, Markov) aplicada a avaliação de parâmetros não-funcionais de sistemas de software em nível arquitetural. Embora IC2 seja parcialmente atendido (uso de Markov), não há evidência de que o método seja aplicado a processos de desenvolvimento de software (modeling de ciclo de vida, CI/CD, event logs). O trabalho mapeia activity diagrams para Petri nets visando análise de performance/confiabilidade do sistema, não a mineração ou modelagem de processos de

title: Mining Collaboration Patterns of Software Development Processes Based on Trace Alignment
abstract: Developing large-scale software usually involves the interaction of a great number of engineers over a long period. To discover the collaboration patterns from developing logs helps improve the software development processes. Traditional techniques of process mining can be employed to identify such patterns. Unfortunately, due to the high uncertainty of software development process, they tend to obtain “spaghetti” models which are difficult to comprehend or even misleading. As a remedy, in this paper we propose an approach to the discovery of collaboration patterns existing in software development process by aligning development logs. It considers not only the sequence of activities, but also the collaboration of actors who perform activities. Instead of using time-consuming graph mining techniques, it employs the trace alignment, which is much more straightforward. Moreover, unlike some traditional approaches, the discovered patterns are determined because we do not depend on the mined process model that is usually uncertain due to the unstructured nature of software development process. The experimental results based on a large dataset generated from CPNTools demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach.
venue: Proceedings of the 2017 International Conference on Software and System Process (ICSSP '17)
ta_rationale: Título indica aplicação de trace alignment (técnica de process mining) a processos de desenvolvimento de software, potencialmente satisfazendo IC1. Porém, ausência de resumo impede confirmação clara do escopo, metodologia e se efetivamente minera eventos/logs de processos de SW ou apenas padrões de colaboração.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica explicitamente process mining (trace alignment) a artefatos e logs de processos de desenvolvimento de software para descobrir padrões de colaboração, atendendo claramente IC1. A abordagem minera event logs de desenvolvimento (desenvolvidos em CPNTools) para identificar padrões de processo, sem EC decisivo aplicável.

title: Mining balanced API protocols
abstract: API protocols can be used in many aspects of software engineering, such as software testing, program validation, software documentation, etc. Mining API protocols based on probabilistic models is proved to be an effective approach to achieve protocols automatically. However, it always achieves unbalanced protocols, that is, protocols described using probabilistic models have unexpected extremely high and low probabilities. In this paper, we discuss the unbalanced probability problem and propose to address it by preprocessing method call sequences used for training. Our method first finds tandem arrays in method call sequences based on the suffix tree. Then, it substitutes each tandem array with a tandem repeat. Since repeated sub method call sequences are eliminated, balanced API protocols may be achieved. In order to investigate the feasibility and effectiveness of our approach, we implemented it in our previous prototype tool ISpecMiner and used the tool to perform a comparison test based on several real-world applications. Experimental results show that our approach can achieve more balanced API protocols than existing approaches, which is essential for mining valid and precise API protocols.
venue: International Journal of Computational Science and Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere mineração de protocolos API, potencialmente relevante para IC1 (process mining em artefatos de SW), mas sem abstract disponível é impossível confirmar se o foco é em processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou em análise estrutural de APIs. A relevância para PATHCAST permanece incerta.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de mineração de protocolos de APIs usando modelos probabilísticos, mas a aplicação é estrutural/de testes, não de processos de desenvolvimento de software (CI/CD, issues, PRs, commits, workflows). A 'mineração' é de padrões de chamadas de método, não de event logs de processos; não há foco em descoberta de processos, conformance checking ou previsão de métricas de processo. Não atende nenhum dos ICs (IC1 exigiria aplicação a artefatos reais de processo de SW como históricos de comm

title: MoProPEI-MP an Adaptation in the selection of process mining tools for software processes
abstract: Process mining is a research discipline that applies data analysis and computational intelligence techniques to extract knowledge from event logs of information systems. It aims to provide new means to discover, monitor, and improve processes. Process mining has gained particular attention over recent years and new process mining software tools, both academic and commercial, have been developed. This paper provides a survey of process mining software tools. It identifies and describes criteria that can be useful for comparing the tools. Furthermore, it introduces a multi-criteria methodology that can be used for the comparative analysis of process mining software tools. The methodology is based on three methods, namely ontology, decision tree, and Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP), that can be used to help users decide which software tool best suits their needs.
venue: Jiisic 2018 Jornadas Iberoamericanas De Ingenieria De Software E Ingenieria Del Conocimiento
ta_rationale: Título fortemente sugere relevância em process mining para processos de software (MoProPEI-MP menciona 'process mining tools for software processes'), potencialmente atendendo IC1. Porém, sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se o artigo realmente aplica técnicas de PM a artefatos/processos de SW, ou se é apenas um framework de seleção de ferramentas desconectado de análises concretas.
ft_rationale: O paper é uma survey/framework de seleção de ferramentas de process mining, não uma aplicação concreta de PM a processos de SW. Não realiza descoberta, conformance, ou análise de event logs de artefatos de software (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD); apenas cataloga e compara ferramentas genéricas. Atende EC4 (revisão secundária fora do escopo).

title: Model-Based Software Product Line Testing by Coupling Feature Models with Hierarchical Markov Chain Usage Models
abstract: Model-based testing automates test case generation based on usage models of a system. In this paper, we introduce an approach for systematic reuse of these models for testing a large family of products. In our approach, we model system usage with hierarchical Markov chains. These models capture all possible usage scenarios for a family of systems. We document variability explicitly and separately with a feature model. We map optional and alternative features in the feature model to a set of transitions in the usage model. Transition probabilities are modified according to the selected features so that the generated test cases focus only on these features. We performed an industrial case study to evaluate the feasibility and efficiency of the approach. We observed that the cost of product line engineering adoption can be amortized with the testing of even a small number of products.
venue: Proceedings 2016 IEEE International Conference on Software Quality Reliability and Security Companion Qrs C 2016
ta_rationale: O título sugere uso de Markov Chain (modelagem estocástica) em contexto de teste de software, potencialmente atendendo IC2. Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se o foco é em análise de processos de desenvolvimento de SW (escopo PATHCAST) ou apenas em testing/verificação de product lines como aplicação de modelos. A ausência de abstract impede avaliação conclusiva.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda model-based testing de product lines usando Markov chains para gerar casos de teste, não análise de processos de desenvolvimento de software. Embora use modelagem estocástica (Markov chains), aplica-se a testing e verificação de sistemas (EC2 — software como ferramenta/técnica), não à mineração, descoberta ou forecasting de processos de desenvolvimento de SW. Não há evidência de process mining, event logs de processos de SW, ou previsão de métricas de processo de desenvolvimento.

title: Practical Reliability and Maintainability Analysis Tool for an Open Source Cloud Computing
abstract: We focus on a cloud computing environment by using open source softwares such as OpenStack and Eucalyptus because of the unification management of data and low cost. A cloud computing is attracting attention as a network service to share the computing resources, that is, networks, servers, storage, applications, and services. We propose jump diffusion models based on stochastic differential equations in order to consider the interesting aspect of the provisioning process. Especially, the reliability and maintainability analysis tool for cloud computing is developed in this paper. Also, we analyze actual data to show numerical illustrations of application of the software analysis tool considering the characteristics of cloud computing. Copyright © 2015 John Wiley & Sons, Ltd.
venue: Quality and Reliability Engineering International
ta_rationale: Título sugere análise de confiabilidade e manutenibilidade em software de código aberto em computação em nuvem, potencialmente envolvendo process mining ou modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento. Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de métricas de processo conforme escopo PATHCAST.
ft_rationale: O paper propõe modelos de difusão de salto baseados em equações diferenciais estocásticas para análise de confiabilidade e manutenibilidade em computação em nuvem. Embora use modelagem estocástica, não aborda process mining, forecasting de métricas de processo de desenvolvimento de SW, ou mineração de repositórios de desenvolvimento. A aplicação é em infraestrutura/provisioning de nuvem, não em processos de engenharia de software (IC2 não se aplica sem evidência de modelagem de processos de dese

title: Predicting Release Time for Open Source Software Based on the Generalized Software Reliability Model
abstract: There is a significant challenge that how to predict the possible release date of the target software having enough reliability in agile development where incremental development and small software releases are key characteristics. Existing approaches targeting agile development usually use release backlogs for predicting and setting delivery windows, however these do not consider the reliability of software for release date prediction so that there is a possibility that software at the predicted release date have poor reliability. Previously we proposed a generalized software reliability model (GSRM) based on a stochastic process and compared it with other models to evaluate recent software developments. However, we, did not directly evaluate the accuracy of the predicted release time by model. In this paper, towards prediction of release dates in agile development, we focus on the release dates of open source software (OSS) developments and the number of detected issues (faults) since OSS developments comply well with the definition of the agile development in terms of incremental process and frequent releases We define the accuracy of the predicted release time using the given development terms and the number of issues. Additionally, we propose a method to evaluate the accuracy of the predicted release time. In the best case, GSRM shows only 0.572% Error Rate, which corresponds to a predicted release date of two days prior to the actual release date. We believe that our method should be applicable to agile developments too.
venue: Proceedings 2015 Agile Conference Agile 2015
ta_rationale: Título sugere forecasting de release time em SW usando modelo de confiabilidade (potencialmente IC3), mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se usa process mining, event logs ou modelagem estocástica adequadamente, ou se foca apenas em confiabilidade/bugs sem abordar processos de desenvolvimento.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica modelagem estocástica (Generalized Software Reliability Model baseado em stochastic process) para forecasting de release time em processos de desenvolvimento de software (agile/OSS). Atende IC3 (forecasting de métricas de processo — release date prediction) e IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW). Não há EC aplicável: o foco é claramente no processo de desenvolvimento de SW, não em confiabilidade isolada ou algoritmo teórico descontextualizado.

title: Process and deviation exploration through Alpha-algorithm and Heuristic miner techniques
abstract: In this paper, we applied two methods of process mining techniques (from Discovery class/approach) in order to extract knowledge from event logs recorded by an online information system. The event log was created via information received from an online proceedings review system in Thailand. Accordingly, Alpha and Heuristic algorithms were used with the objective of automatically visualizing the models in terms of Petri nets and animated simulations. The paper eventually aimed at improving the handling of online reviews by providing techniques and tools for discovering process, control, data, organizational, and social structures from the created event log.
venue: International Conference on ICT and Knowledge Engineering
ta_rationale: Título menciona técnicas de process mining (Alpha-algorithm, Heuristic miner) relevantes para IC1, mas sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se aplicadas a processos de SW ou outro domínio. A conferência é genérica em ICT, aumentando incerteza sobre o escopo específico.
ft_rationale: Embora o resumo confirme uso de process mining (Alpha e Heuristic miner) com descoberta de processo, a aplicação é em um sistema de revisão de proceedings (conferência) em tailândia, não em processos de desenvolvimento de software. O contexto é gestão de revisão de artigos científicos, não engenharia de software, o que viola o escopo IC1 que requer aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou processos de desenvolvimento. EC1 é claramente aplicável: domínio fora de SW.

title: Process mining event logs from FLOSS data: State of the art and perspectives
abstract: Free/Libre Open Source Software (FLOSS) is a phenomenon that has undoubtedly triggered extensive research endeavors. At the heart of these initiatives is the ability to mine data from FLOSS repositories with the hope of revealing empirical evidence to answer existing questions on the FLOSS development process. In spite of the success produced with existing mining techniques, emerging questions about FLOSS data require alternative and more appropriate ways to explore and analyse such data. In this paper, we explore a different perspective called process mining. Process mining has been proved to be successful in terms of tracing and reconstructing process models from data logs (event logs). The chief objective of our analysis is threefold. We aim to achieve: (1) conformance to predefined models; (2) discovery of new model patterns; and, finally, (3) extension to predefined models
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: Título fortemente sugestivo de process mining em SW (FLOSS/open source) que atende potencialmente IC1, mas ausência de resumo impede avaliação conclusiva da metodologia, escopo exato e aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software vs. análise genérica de repositórios.
ft_rationale: O paper atende claramente IC1 ao aplicar process mining (discovery, conformance, extension) a artefatos de FLOSS/open source software (event logs de repositórios). A proposta explícita é usar process mining para descobrir e analisar modelos de processo de desenvolvimento de software a partir de dados de repositórios, que é o cerne da intersecção entre process mining e engenharia de software.

title: Social network analysis and role hierarchy mining through MXML-based event logs
abstract: This paper is divided into three main parts. In the first part of the study, we initially collected a process event log -from an information system -during an Academic Writing (English) training course in Thailand. The original data was consisted of 330 process instances and 3,326 events in total. Being aware of the fact that ProM process mining framework accepts and supports only XES and MXML logs, the data was subsequently configured and converted into the fitting format. In the second part of the study, we applied role hierarchy mining technique in order to evaluate the role of every individual throughout the course/training program. Moreover, the organizational mining technique enabled us to better understand the functional structure of the training organization (based on models) and ultimately improve (or benchmark) the underlying processes. In the thirst part of the study, we used social network mining as a pre-requisite for social network analysis to discover the interaction patterns between the originators during the teaching process. Accordingly, the handover of work metric -of the social network mining technique-made possible to not only investigate direct relationships but also indirect relationships as well.
venue: International Conference on ICT and Knowledge Engineering
ta_rationale: O título sugere análise de event logs em formato MXML (mining markup language), indicando potencial relevância para process mining (IC1). Contudo, sem resumo disponível, é impossível confirmar se a aplicação é específica a processos de software ou a domínios genéricos de BPM (EC1), e se a análise de redes sociais/hierarquia é orientada ao descobrimento/modelagem de processos de SW ou apenas a extração de estruturas organizacionais.
ft_rationale: Embora o paper aplique process mining (descoberta de roles, organizational mining, social network analysis) a event logs em formato MXML, a aplicação é a um curso de treinamento em escrita acadêmica (domínio educacional genérico), não a processos de desenvolvimento de software (EC1). A análise de hierarquia de papéis e interações organizacionais não está orientada a artefatos de SW ou processos de desenvolvimento, desalinhando-se do escopo PATHCAST.

title: The use of process mining in business process simulation model construction structuring the field
abstract: The paper focuses on the use of process mining (PM) to support the construction of business process simulation (BPS) models. Given the useful BPS insights that are available in event logs, further research on this topic is required. To provide a solid basis for future work, this paper presents a structured overview of BPS modeling tasks and how PM can support them. As directly related research efforts are scarce, a multitude of research challenges are identified. In an effort to provide suggestions on how these challenges can be tackled, an analysis of PM literature shows that few PM algorithms are directly applicable in a BPS context. Consequently, the results presented in this paper can encourage and guide future research to fundamentally bridge the gap between PM and BPS.business process simulation; process mining;e vent log knowledge; simulation model constructio
venue: Business and Information Systems Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere intersecção entre process mining e simulação de processos de negócio, potencialmente relevante para IC2 (modelagem estocástica) ou IC1 (process mining). Porém, sem abstract disponível, não é possível confirmar se o foco é em processos de software ou apenas BPM genérico, e se há aplicação concreta em SE.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de process mining aplicado a simulação de processos de negócio (BPS) genericamente, sem evidência de foco em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Nenhum IC é atendido: IC1 requer aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD), IC2 requer modelagem estocástica de processos de SW, IC3 requer forecasting de métricas de processo de SW, e IC4 requer mineração de repositórios de SW. O resumo indica escopo em BPM genérico ('business process simulation model construction') s

title: Towards a Bayesian Decision Model for Release Planning in Incremental Development
abstract: Incremental software development focuses on delivering working software in small increments so as to deliver early business value and minimize the risks of developing inadequate system requirements. Release planning is the activity that consist in planning what features and software qualities will be delivered in each release. Release planning decisions are complex due to conflicting stakeholders' objectives, uncertainty, and complex project constraints. Judgement-based approaches to release planning decisions are commonly adopted in practice but are subject to severe biases and lack of transparency. Model-based approaches have been proposed to address these problems but most methods use generic models that do not fit stakeholders' concerns and provide limited support for modelling and reasoning about uncertainty. The objective of this research is to propose a Bayesian approach to software release planning that uses economic and problem-specific, falsifiable models to support release planning decisions under uncertainty. Our approach uses Monte-Carlo simulation and multi-objective optimization to identify release plans that maximize net present value and minimize risks.
venue: Proceedings 2017 IEEE 25th International Requirements Engineering Conference Re 2017
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (Bayesian) aplicada a processo de SW (release planning em desenvolvimento incremental), potencialmente atendendo IC2. Contudo, sem abstract disponível, não é possível confirmar se o foco é realmente no processo de desenvolvimento ou se é apenas uma ferramenta de decisão genérica. A ausência de resumo impede validação clara do escopo e aplicação empírica.
ft_rationale: Embora o paper use modelagem estocástica (Bayesian, Monte Carlo) em contexto de software, o foco é em ferramenta de decisão (otimização econômica) para release planning, não em mineração, análise ou modelagem de processos reais de desenvolvimento de software a partir de dados/logs. O paper não atende IC2 (que requer modelagem de processos de SW reais com Markov/estocástico), nem IC1, IC3 ou IC4, pois não há: descoberta de processo, análise de event logs, forecasting de métricas de processo, ou m

title: Using event log knowledge to support business process simulation model construction
venue: Ceur Workshop Proceedings
ta_rationale: O título sugere forte relevância para IC1 (process mining em artefatos de software via event logs para construção de modelos de processo). Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se o escopo é restrito a SW ou se aborda domínios genéricos de BPM. A data (2018) e veículo (CEUR Workshop) indicam potencial relevância, mas falta clareza sobre aplicação específica a processos de SW.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de simulação de processos de negócio genéricos (produção, faturação, callcenter) usando event logs. Embora aplique process mining (IC1 potencial), não há evidência de aplicação específica a processos de desenvolvimento de software (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD, etc.). O domínio é Business Process Management genérico, não Engenharia de Software, caracterizando EC1 (domínio fora de SW).

title: A Case Study: To Discuss the Construction of the Finite State Machine and Markov-Chain OP Models for Test Case Generation
abstract: Finite State Machine (FSM) is used to solve problems in math, games, and practical skills by making use of regions and transformers to model individual processing units and link them to create a complete sequence of operating conditions, which can be handled easily. Owing to their logical structure, FSMs are considered to be ideal tools for structural and behavioral modeling of software applications that assist in various phases of SDLC(Software Design Life Cycle) like Requirement Specifications, Design, Testing, etc. Software testing cost is a function of the size of the constructed test suite, given as, the number of tests and their combined length. FSMs are deployed as a smart tool to get a set of prioritized test suits that not only verify the user value-based system features rather reduce the testing cost to a major extent. This paper aims to provide comprehensive knowledge about what is FSM (Finite State Machine) and how it can be incorporated to model some software applications. For further illustration, a case study of the Daraz App has been discussed for which both simple and Markovian Operational Profiles (MOP) have been formed using the Mealy models for the graphical, tabular, and list representations. We aim that this study proves to be a productive contribution for individuals working with different types of FSMs to get an in-depth understanding of model construction and its implication for software testing.
venue: 2024 5th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences Icacs 2024
ta_rationale: O título sugere uso de Finite State Machine e Markov-Chain para geração de casos de teste, potencialmente alinhado com IC2 (modelagem estocástica). Contudo, sem abstract não é possível confirmar se o foco é em processos de SW (como CI/CD, desenvolvimento) ou em teste como domínio isolado. ft_rationale: O artigo foca em modelagem de FSM e Markov-Chain para geração de casos de teste (testing) em uma aplicação (Daraz App), não em process mining, descoberta de processos de desenvolvimento de software, ou forecasting de métricas de processo. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) nem modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento. A estocástica é aplicada a operações de teste, não a processos de SW. Nenhum IC é atendido; EC2 (SW como ferramenta/domínio de teste isol

title: A Divide-and-Conquer Approach for Modeling Arrival Times in Business Process Simulation
abstract: Business Process Simulation (BPS) is a critical tool for analyzing and improving organizational processes by estimating the impact of process changes. A key component of BPS is the case-arrival model, which determines the pattern of new case entries into a process. Although accurate case-arrival modeling is essential for reliable simulations, as it influences waiting and overall cycle times, existing approaches often rely on oversimplified static distributions of inter-arrival times. These approaches fail to capture the dynamic and temporal complexities inherent in organizational environments, leading to less accurate and reliable outcomes. To address this limitation, we propose Auto Time Kernel Density Estimation (AT-KDE), a divide-and-conquer approach that models arrival times of processes by incorporating global dynamics, day-of-week variations, and intraday distributional changes, ensuring both precision and scalability. Experiments conducted across 20 diverse processes demonstrate that AT-KDE is far more accurate and robust than existing approaches while maintaining sensible execution time efficiency.
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica de processos (arrival times, simulation), potencialmente relevante para IC2, mas sem resumo não é possível confirmar se aplica especificamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software ou se é BPM genérico fora do escopo.

Embora aborde modelagem estocástica (potencialmente IC2), não há evidência no resumo de aplicação específica a processos de desenvolvimento de software (commits, issues, CI/CD, etc.). O contexto é BPM/organizational processes, não SE. Enquadra-se em EC1 (domínio fora de SW) — a modelagem é genérica e os 20 processos estudados não são identificados como pr

title: A Framework for Explainable Process Mining (XPM): Steps Toward Adopting AI Transparency Principles Applied to Process Mining
abstract: Process mining has emerged as a vital tool for analyzing and optimizing workflows in various domains. However, traditional process mining approaches often lack transparency and interpretability, limiting their effectiveness in addressing complex decis

title: A Hybrid Approach to Process Mining: Finding Immediate Successors of a Process by Using From-To Chart
abstract: Process mining is a branch of data mining that aims to discover process model from the event logs. In this study, we propose a hybrid approach to process mining in such a way that, "from-to chart" is used as the frontend to monitor the transitions among activities of a realistic event log. Another novelty of this study is developed evaluation metrics, which are used for finding immediate successors in order to convert these raw relations into dependency/frequency graph.
venue: 2009 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications
keywords: Monitoring; Machine learning; Production; Software engineering; Neural networks; Noise measurement; Concurrent computing; Connectors; Petri nets; Machine learning algorithms; From-to Chart; Process Discovery; Immediate Successor; Process Mining; Event Logs
ta_rationale: O artigo descreve uma abordagem de process mining com descoberta de modelo a partir de event logs e métricas de avaliação para relações de dependência. Embora aborde process mining (IC1), o resumo não especifica claramente se a aplicação é em processos de software ou em domínios genéricos, criando ambiguidade sobre atendimento ao escopo da SLR. Não há evidência explícita de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou repositórios.
ft_rationale: O artigo apresenta uma abordagem genérica de process mining (descoberta de modelo, from-to chart, dependency graph) sem qualquer evidência no resumo de aplicação específica a processos de desenvolvimento de software, artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou repositórios. Atende formalmente IC1 em abstrato, mas falha no requisito implícito de aplicação ao domínio de SE. Sem avaliação ou exemplos em SW relatados no resumo, incorre em EC2 (SW como ferramenta genérica, não como domínio de est

title: A Method for Debugging Process Discovery Pipelines to Analyze the Consistency of Model Properties
abstract: Event logs have become a valuable information source for business process management, e.g., when analysts discover process models to inspect the process behavior and to infer actionable insights. To this end, analysts configure discovery pipelines in which logs are filtered, enriched, abstracted, and process models are derived. While pipeline operations are necessary to manage log imperfections and complexity, they might, however, influence the nature of the discovered process model and its properties. Ultimately, not considering this possibility can negatively affect downstream decision making. We hence propose a framework for assessing the consistency of model properties with respect to the pipeline operations and their parameters, and, if inconsistencies are present, for revealing which parameters contribute to them. Following recent literature on software engineering for machine learning, we refer to it as debugging. From evaluating our framework in a real-world analysis scenario based on complex event logs and third-party pipeline configurations, we see strong evidence towards it being a valuable addition to the process mining toolbox
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: O título sugere relevância a process discovery (potencialmente IC1), mas a ausência de abstract impede avaliação definitiva sobre aplicação a processos de software. O escopo pode ser BPM genérico sem conexão a SE.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de debugging de pipelines de process discovery em contexto genérico de Business Process Management (BPM). Embora aborde process mining (descoberta de processos), não há evidência no resumo de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software (artefatos como commits, issues, PR, CI/CD). O domínio é BPM genérico sem conexão clara a SE, caracterizando EC1 (domínio fora de SW).

title: A Two-Level Approach Based on Model Checking to Support Architecture Conformance Checking
abstract: Formal methods are fundamental to ensure that software systems behave as expected. These methods are used to identify errors in system design and to certificate that systems follow the proper requirements. In Software Engineering, preserving the correspondence between the design architecture and the architecture indeed implemented by software developers is a crucial issue. Architecture Conformance Checking is the process of checking if these two architectures are in accordance along the software development course. We propose a Model Checking based method to aid Architecture Conformance Checking, which is a fundamental analysis to ensure software quality, dependability and maintainability. In this work, a new logic, which combines temporal logic, hybrid logic and a new logical operator in order to formalize software specifications, is proposed. The method described in this paper uses two structures, namely call graphs and software version graphs. The first one is used to check specifications related to classes and methods and we apply it intending to analyze a specific software version. The latter one gives us an overview of the software development process and we employ it to check global software requirements. These two graphs allow us to design a two-level checking method. The first level deals with specifications of a single software version that must be inspected in the call graph. The second level handles the global requirements throughout all software versions. Using our new operator and a function, we are able to use the same logic in both levels, allowing them to communicate with each other and handle the verification process in a neat and uniform manner. Our two-level approach is the great differential of this work, since the current approaches available in the literature focus on an unique software version at a time. We also present an algorithm, which has polynomial time complexity, to perform Model Checking for our proposed temporal logic.
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: Título sugere conformance checking (potencialmente relacionado a IC1 - process mining em SW), mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se trata de processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou apenas arquitetura de sistemas. A abordagem pode ser puramente técnica (EC3) sem avaliação em event logs ou repositórios de SW.
ft_rationale: O paper propõe um método de model checking para conformance checking de arquitetura de software, focando em verificação formal de propriedades em call graphs e software version graphs. Não há evidência de aplicação a process mining (IC1), modelagem estocástica (IC2), forecasting de métricas de processo (IC3) ou mineração de repositórios (IC4). O trabalho é sobre verificação de conformidade arquitetural, não sobre descoberta ou análise de processos de desenvolvimento de SW em event logs ou reposi

title: A Use Case Analysis assessing Process Mining Model Quality for Simulation
abstract: Ever-evolving business processes can be re-engineered with process mining and simulation as a technology, as the approach shapes future outcomes, analyzing their present and past. Developing effective models can make it easier and more cost-effective, as there are potential benefits such as working with what-if scenarios and eliminating bottlenecks, but their quality check is always an issue. Different criteria have been applied to tackle this quality issue, disclosing expected benefits and weaknesses. This can help build suitable ground for analyzing and implementing relevant use cases. The criteria extracted from process model quality research demonstrate the effectiveness of model assessment and have practical implications for improving use case modeling. Results display the quality of particular models and indicate how use case modeling can be improved
venue: Lecture Notes in Informatics Lni Proceedings Series of the Gesellschaft Fur Informatik Gi
ta_rationale: O título sugere potencial relevância a process mining e simulação, possivelmente atendendo IC1 ou IC2, mas a ausência de abstract impede confirmação clara do domínio (SW vs. domínio genérico) e da aplicabilidade aos critérios de inclusão.

title: A method for software reliability test case design based on Markov chain usage model
abstract: Test case design is a key factor to improve software reliability level. A new method for software reliability test case design based on Markov chain usage model is presented. Construction steps of software Markov chain usage Model based on UML are introduced. Markov chain usage model is described with directed graph. Auto generation arithmetic of reliability test case is proposed to ease the software reliability test in practice. Based on the method, an ATM software reliability test case is designed and demonstrated. The result proves the method in this paper is practical and efficient in engineering practices.
venue: Qr2mse 2013 Proceedings of 2013 International Conference on Quality Reliability Risk Maintenance and Safety Engineering
ta_rationale: O título sugere uso de Markov chain para modelagem de processos de software (teste e confiabilidade), potencialmente atendendo IC2. Porém, sem abstract disponível, não é possível confirmar se o foco é realmente em processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou em confiabilidade de produtos software como artefato final, e se há análise de event logs ou dados reais de processo.
ft_rationale: O paper foca em design de casos de teste para confiabilidade de software usando cadeias de Markov como modelo de uso (produto), não em mineração ou modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento de SW. Não há evidência de análise de event logs, repositórios, ou processos de engenharia de software (commits, issues, CI/CD, etc.). IC2 (modelagem estocástica de processos de SW) não se aplica pois o Markov chain modela o comportamento esperado do software testado, não o processo de desenvolvimento. EC2 (s

title: A method for stochastic modeling the software development process in constrained resource environments
abstract: Process Improvement requires a formal process definition and associated measures of performance. Processes can then be diagnosed in order to formulate hypotheses on where changes should be made. Subsequently, new processes can be conceived, and developers can check whether the desired improvements have actually been achieved. Simulation is a powerful low-cost tool for diagnosis and test of several improvement alternatives, prior to field tests. This paper presents a UML-based method to obtain the probability distribution of the execution time of a large variety of business processes, including software development. Such a method, which is based on Monte Carlo simulation, allows for the identification of factors that most strongly influence process execution time, favoring changes that increase process efficiency with considerable impact on the deployment of business tactics and strategies.
venue: Proceedings of the 2008 IEEE Systems and Information Engineering Design Symposium Sieds 2008
ta_rationale: O título sugere fortemente atendimento a IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW), mas a ausência de resumo impede confirmação do escopo real e aplicação efetiva ao domínio de SW. Não há clareza se o artigo trata realmente processos de desenvolvimento de software ou apenas software como ferramenta de modelagem.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente IC2: apresenta método de modelagem estocástica (Monte Carlo simulation) aplicado especificamente a processos de desenvolvimento de software (software development process), com objetivo de diagnóstico e melhoria de processo. Não há EC aplicável — software é o domínio sendo estudado, não ferramenta.

title: A simulation model for process-centered software engineering environments using sensitivity analysis
abstract: Software development is a high risk activity that requires planning to reduce costs and meet deadlines. This paper presents a simulation model, integrated to a tool that supports project management centered processes, uses the information in the PSEE database, offers resources to manipulate process data in a deterministic or stochastic way (simulation), allows testing various scenarios and identifies the variables that have most impact on the final result (sensitivity analysis). The results achieved by implementing this model, using the information of a real case study, were able to provide important data to improve the process under study.
venue: Proceedings 2011 Workshop School on Theoretical Computer Science Weit 2011
ta_rationale: O título sugere forte relevância para IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW) com menção explícita a 'simulation model' e 'process-centered software engineering environments'. Contudo, sem acesso ao resumo, não é possível confirmar se o trabalho efetivamente utiliza técnicas estocásticas (Markov, Monte Carlo, etc.) ou apenas simulação genérica, nem se aplica análise de sensibilidade a artefatos reais de SW ou a modelos teóricos abstratos.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma explicitamente a implementação de um modelo de simulação integrado a um ambiente de engenharia de software centrado em processos, com capacidade de manipular dados de forma determinística ou estocástica. A abordagem usa dados reais de um estudo de caso e análise de sensibilidade para identificar variáveis de impacto, alinhando-se diretamente com IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW). title: A vector Markov model for structural coverage growth and the number of failure occurrences
abstract: Most software reliability growth models specify the expected number of failures experienced as a function of testing effort or calendar time. However, there are approaches to model the development of intermediate factors driving failure occurrences. This paper starts out with presenting a model framework consisting of four consecutive relationships. It is shown that a differential equation representing this framework is a generalization of several finite failures category models. The relationships between the number of test cases executed and expected structural coverage, and between expected structural coverage and the expected number of failure occurrences are then explored further. A vector Markov model allowing for partial redundancy in sampling code constructs is developed. The model bridges the gap between setups related to operational testing and systematic testing, respectively. Two extensions of the partial redundancy model considering the development of the number of failure occurrences are discussed. The paper concludes with showing that the extended models fit into the structure of the differential equation presented at the beginning, which permits further interpretation.
venue: Proceedings International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering ISSRE
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (Markov) aplicada a métricas de software (cobertura estrutural e falhas), potencialmente relevante para IC2. Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se o foco é em modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou em modelagem de confiabilidade/teste de software como artefato.

Embora use Markov (IC2), não aplica isso a modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento (workflow, CI/CD, gestão de tarefas), mas sim a artefatos de teste como cobertura estrutural e ocorrência de falhas. Cai sob EC2 — software é objeto de teste, não o processo sendo estudado.

title: ARCH-COMP25 Category Report: Stochastic Models
abstract: This report is concerned with a friendly competition for formal verification and policy synthesis of stochastic models. The main goal of the report is to introduce new benchmarks and their properties within this category and recommend next steps toward next year’s edition of the competition. In particular, this report introduces three recently developed software tools, a new water distribution network benchmark, and a collection of simplified benchmarks intended to facilitate further comparisons among tools that were previously not directly comparable. This friendly competition took place as part of the workshop Applied Verification for Continuous and Hybrid Systems (ARCH) in Summer 2025.
venue: Epic Series in Computing

ft_rationale: O paper trata de competição amigável para verificação formal e síntese de política em modelos estocásticos genéricos, com foco em benchmarks e ferramentas de análise formal. Não há evidência de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software, repositórios, artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou modelagem de processos de engenharia de software. Enquadra-se em EC1 (domínio fora de SW) e EC3 (algoritmo/ferramenta teórica sem avaliação em processos de SW).

title: Agile Development with Software Process Mining
authors: Rubin, Vladimir; Lomazova, Irina; van der Aalst, Wil M. P.
abstract: Modern companies continue investing more and more in the creation, maintenance and change of software systems, but the proper specification and design of such systems continues to be a challenge. The majority of current approaches either ignore real user and system runtime behavior or consider it only informally. This leads to a rather prescriptive top-down approach to software development.
venue: Proceedings of the 2014 International Conference on Software and System Process (ICSSP '14)
ta_rationale: Título altamente relevante sugere aplicação de process mining a desenvolvimento ágil de software (IC1), porém a ausência de resumo impede confirmação definitiva do escopo e metodologia. Revista (ICSSP) é apropriada para o tema.
