title: Measuring reliability growth of open source software by applying Stochastic Differential Equations
abstract: This, paper presents (i) several software reliability, growth models (SRGM) which tries to predict, quantitatively the failure, phenomena, in, an, Open, Source, Software, project over a period of time. Here, it is assumed that the number of failures during testing is dependent upon the number of instructions execution, (ii) in order to cater the, irregular state, of, bug-report, phenomena, on, the, bug tracking system and irregular fluctuation in terms of noise, the reliability models have been proposed by applying an Itô type Stochastic Differential Equations (SDE). We have demonstrated that proposed models can support management in building reliable software systems by predicting remaining bugs. We have also compared our proposed models with several existing models on the basis of different comparison criteria.
venue: Proceedings 2010 2nd Wri World Congress on Software Engineering Wcse 2010
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (SDEs) aplicada a software de código aberto, potencialmente alinhado com IC2. Porém, sem abstract disponível é impossível confirmar se o foco é realmente em processos de desenvolvimento de SW (IC2) ou apenas em confiabilidade/defeitos do produto final (fora do escopo de processos).
ft_rationale: O paper aplica Stochastic Differential Equations (SDEs) para modelar crescimento de confiabilidade e prever bugs remanescentes em software de código aberto. Porém, o foco é em confiabilidade do produto final e previsão de falhas/defeitos, não em processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC2 requer modelagem estocástica de processos de SW como CI/CD, workflows de desenvolvimento). Não há evidência de análise de event logs, descoberta de processos ou modelagem de fluxos de desenvolvimento — critér

title: Mining behavior models from user-intensive web applications
abstract: Many modern user-intensive applications, such as Web applications, must satisfy the interaction requirements of thousands if not millions of users, which can be hardly fully understood at design time. Designing applications that meet user behaviors, by efficiently supporting the prevalent navigation patterns, and evolving with them requires new approaches that go beyond classic software engineering solutions. We present a novel approach that automates the acquisition of user-interaction requirements in an incremental and reflective way. Our solution builds upon inferring a set of probabilistic Markov models of the users' navigational behaviors, dynamically extracted from the interaction history given in the form of a log file. We annotate and analyze the inferred models to verify quantitative properties by means of probabilistic model checking. The paper investigates the advantages of the approach referring to a Web application currently in use.
venue: Proceedings International Conference on Software Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere mineração de modelos de comportamento em aplicações web, potencialmente relevante para descoberta de processos (IC1), mas sem resumo não é possível confirmar se o foco é em processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou em comportamento de usuários em aplicações web (EC2 - SW como ferramenta, não processo sendo estudado).
ft_rationale: O artigo aplica modelagem estocástica (Markov) a logs de interação de usuários em aplicações web, não a artefatos ou processos de desenvolvimento de software (commits, issues, CI/CD, VCS). O foco é em comportamento de navegação de usuários finais, caracterizando EC2 (software como ferramenta/plataforma, não processo de SW sendo estudado). Nenhum IC é claramente atendido.

title: Model-based scheduling analysis for software projects
abstract: We show how to compute optimal policies for the scheduling of software development projects under uncertainty. Our approach is based on a stochastic scheduling model that explicitly captures the strong feedback between the tasks in software development ("ripple effects"). We apply reinforcement learning to the optimization problem. For a selected sample project, we compute the optimal policy, simulate the project, and analyze the task assignments that are made by the optimal policy. From the analysis of the simulated schedules, we derive tentative, generic scheduling guidelines. The guidelines prioritize certain tasks based on the characteristics of the software architecture, and assign tasks according to the past performance of the developers.
venue: Proceedings International Computer Software and Applications Conference
ta_rationale: O título sugere análise de agendamento em projetos de software usando modelo baseado em modelos, o que pode indicar modelagem estocástica ou análise de processo de SW (IC2). Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, forecasting de métricas ou mineria de repositórios, nem descartar que seja puramente teórico (EC3).
ft_rationale: O paper aborda modelagem estocástica (scheduling model) e otimização (reinforcement learning) aplicada a projetos de software, o que poderia sugerir IC2. Porém, o foco é em otimização de alocação de tarefas e políticas de agendamento sob incerteza, não em descoberta/conformance de processos reais (IC1), forecasting de métricas de processo a partir de event logs (IC3), ou mineração de repositórios (IC4). Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos reais de SW (commits, issues, logs de eventos) nem

title: Model-based time estimate of software test processes
abstract: In order to plan and manage the process of software test, it is necessary to estimate the test process time exactly. An improved process time estimate model was proposed based on analyzing other process models. The model takes into account the effects of rework possibilities and rework impact factors on the process time. What makes the proposed model different from others is that the rework impact factors in this model affect the remaining time of the rework task other than the task duration. The time estimate methods are illustrated by an example of a software test process. The analysis of the models and the comparison between them are presented.
venue: 2007 International Conference on Wireless Communications Networking and Mobile Computing Wicom 2007
ta_rationale: O título sugere estimativa de tempo em processos de teste de software, potencialmente relevante para IC3 (forecasting de métricas de processo). Contudo, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se usa modelagem estocástica, process mining ou event logs, ou se o domínio é efetivamente engenharia de software (venue em conferência de wireless/mobile computing levanta dúvida sobre escopo).
ft_rationale: O resumo descreve um modelo teórico de estimativa de tempo para processos de teste de software, focado em rework e impacto de fatores. Não há evidência de process mining (IC1), modelagem estocástica com métodos formais como Markov/Petri nets (IC2), forecasting baseado em dados históricos ou event logs (IC3), ou mineração de repositórios (IC4). O modelo proposto é analítico/matemático, não baseado em análise de dados de processos reais. Além disso, a venue (Wireless Communications Networking and

title: Model-driven evolution of software architectures
abstract: This paper gives an overview of a Ph.D.-project that investigates approaches to support the evolution of software architectures. Particularly, we focus on the use of model-driven techniques in the context of software product-lines. We recognize four tasks related to software evolution: evaluation, conformance checking, migration, and documentation. We propose model-driven solutions for these tasks. By their application in industrial case studies we investigate the extent to which the evolution tasks can be automated, the impact of the use of software product lines, and the possibilities and difficulties to integrate these solutions in industrial practice
venue: Proceedings of the European Conference on Software Maintenance and Reengineering CSMR
ta_rationale: O título sugere foco em evolução de arquitetura de software, não em process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de processos. Sem abstract disponível, não é possível determinar se o trabalho aborda análise de processos de desenvolvimento (event logs, repositórios) ou apenas transformação estrutural de código/arquitetura.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de evolução de arquitetura de software usando técnicas model-driven e product-lines, focando em tarefas como avaliação, conformance checking, migração e documentação de arquiteturas. Não aborda process mining (descoberta de processos a partir de event logs), modelagem estocástica de processos de desenvolvimento, forecasting de métricas de processo, ou mineração de repositórios para descoberta de modelos de processo. O trabalho é sobre transformação e evolução estrutural de sistemas

title: Modeling and predicting software failure costs
abstract: For software, the costs of failures are not clearly understood. Often, these costs disappear in the costs of testing, the general developments costs, or the operating expenses. In a general manufacturing context, the British standard BS-6143-2:1990 classifies quality-related costs into prevention costs, appraisal costs, and failure costs. It furthermore recommends to identify the activities carried out within each of these categories, and to measure the costs connected with the activities. The standard thus presents a framework for recording and structuring costs once they have occurred. In this paper, we propose an approach for structuring the information on internal and external software failure costs such that their development over time can be represented by stochastic models. Based on these models, future failure costs can be predicted. In two case studies we show how the approach was applied in an industrial software development project.
venue: Proceedings International Computer Software and Applications Conference
ta_rationale: O título sugere modelagem e predição de custos de falhas de software, o que poderia envolver forecasting de métricas de processo (IC3). Porém, sem abstract, é incerto se o estudo: (a) foca em processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou apenas em falhas/confiabilidade de código; (b) usa event logs, process mining ou modelagem estocástica; (c) analisa processos de SW ou apenas características de software como artefato. Necessário acesso ao texto completo para decisão conclusiva.
ft_rationale: O paper propõe modelagem estocástica (stochastic models) para prever custos de falhas de software ao longo do tempo, mas não aborda process mining, event logs de processos de desenvolvimento, ou forecasting de métricas de processo de desenvolvimento (lead time, cycle time, throughput). O foco é em custos de falha e confiabilidade de software como artefato, não em processos de desenvolvimento de SW (IC1, IC3, IC4 não se aplicam). IC2 é parcialmente relevante (modelagem estocástica), mas sem ligaç

title: Monitoring the Software Development Process with Process Mining
venue: Lecture Notes in Business Information Processing
ta_rationale: Título altamente relevante sugere aplicação de process mining a processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC1), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo, metodologia e resultados. Necessária leitura do texto completo para decisão definitiva.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica process mining a artefatos de software (VCS, issue trackers) para descobrir e monitorar o processo real de desenvolvimento de software, atendendo diretamente IC1. Não há evidência de exclusão (EC). É um position paper que estabelece a ponte entre process mining e mining software repositories, exatamente dentro do escopo PATHCAST.

title: Monte Carlo simulation based estimations: Case from a global outsourcing company
abstract: Effort estimation of various software project life cycle phases have been traditionally performed using deterministic methods. Deterministic methods fail to account for the variability inherent in the software engineering processes deployed in executing the project. We have developed stochastic notations and models that can represent these process variations. We have also constructed a Monte Carlo simulation tool that works using the stochastic model we have developed for software development projects. This tool has been tested in a mid-sized software services outsourcing (SSO) organization. The tool and results are described and reported in this paper.
venue: Proceedings of the 1st International Technology Management Conference Itmc 2011
ta_rationale: Título sugere aplicação de Monte Carlo para estimações em empresa de outsourcing global, potencialmente alinhado com IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW). Contudo, sem abstract não é possível confirmar se o domínio é desenvolvimento de software, se as estimações referem-se a métricas de processo de SW, ou se é aplicação genérica de simulação em contexto empresarial.
ft_rationale: O resumo confirma claramente IC2: desenvolve notações e modelos estocásticos (Markov implícito em simulação Monte Carlo) para representar variações em processos de engenharia de software, e constrói ferramenta de simulação Monte Carlo testada em organização de desenvolvimento de software. Aplicação concreta a processos de desenvolvimento (fases do ciclo de vida de projetos de software) sem EC decisivo.

title: Multiple approaches integration for computer-supported software development
abstract: The paper focuses on the actual problem of development of methods and tools for processes simulation and software engineering. Authors developed software that lets automate the whole process starting with the processes formalization, business processes and decision making processes dynamic models development, carrying out simulation experiments with the models, bottlenecks analysis, process re-engineering and optimization, and finishing with software engineering, which includes database and software modules generation. During research many available CASE tools were studied, analysed and searched for disadvantages. Particularly, most of the tools lacked structural and object-oriented approaches integration and design process intelligence, i.e. no interaction between various kinds of UML diagrams. Keeping in mind these factors and considering small performance on complicated systems analysis utilizing simulation modeling a distributed intelligent multi-agent resource conversion processes based CASE tool was engineered.
venue: 2009 Chinese Control and Decision Conference Ccdc 2009
ta_rationale: Título sugere integração de abordagens para desenvolvimento de software, potencialmente relevante a processos de SW, mas sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se atende critérios de inclusão (IC1-IC4) ou se incorre em exclusões (EC1-EC4). Venue em controle e decisão (2009 CCDC) também cria ambiguidade sobre o foco real.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de desenvolvimento de ferramentas CASE e simulação de processos de negócio em geral, sem foco específico em process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting de métricas ou mineração de repositórios de software. A simulação mencionada é genérica (dinâmica de modelos de processos), não ligada a event logs, descoberta de processos ou predição em artefatos de desenvolvimento de software (commits, issues, CI/CD). Nenhum dos critérios de inclusão (IC1-IC4) é claramente atendido.

title: ON SYSTEM COMPLEXITY, STABILITY AND PERFORMANCE: APPLICATION TO PREDICTION
venue: Mathematics and Mechanics of Complex Systems
ta_rationale: Título sugere foco em complexidade de sistemas e predição, potencialmente relevante para IC3 (forecasting), mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se trata processos de SW ou domínio genérico. Venue (Mathematics and Mechanics) indica possível foco matemático abstrato fora do escopo de engenharia de software.
ft_rationale: Resumo ausente e venue (Mathematics and Mechanics of Complex Systems) indica foco em teoria matemática abstrata de sistemas complexos, sem evidência de aplicação a processos de software. Título genérico sobre 'system complexity, stability and performance' não fornece indicadores confiáveis de relevância ao escopo PATHCAST (process mining + SW). Sem conteúdo concreto, impossível confirmar nenhum IC.

title: Obtaining thresholds for the effectiveness of business process mining
abstract: Business process mining is a powerful tool to retrieve the valuable business knowledge embedded in existing information systems. The effectiveness of this kind of proposal is usually evaluated using recall and precision, which respectively measure the completeness and exactness of the retrieved business processes. Since the effectiveness assessment of business process mining is a difficult and error-prone activity, the main hypothesis of this work studies the possibility of obtaining thresholds to determine when recall and precision values are appropriate. The business process mining technique under study is MARBLE, a model-driven framework to retrieve business processes from existing information systems. The Bender method was applied to obtain the thresholds of the recall and precision measures. The experimental data used as input were obtained from a set of 44 business processes retrieved with MARBLE through a family of case studies carried out over the last two years. The study provides thresholds for recall and precision measures, which facilitates the interpretation of their values by means of five linguistic labels that range from low to very high. As a result, recall must be high (with at least a medium precision above 0.56), and precision must also be high (with at least a low recall of 0.70) to ensure that business processes were recovered (by using MARBLE) with an effectiveness value above 0.65. The thresholds allowed us to ascertain with more confidence whether MARBLE can effectively mine business processes from existing information systems. In addition, the provided results can be used as reference values to compare MARBLE with other similar business process mining techniques.
venue: International Symposium on Empirical Software Engineering and Measurement
ta_rationale: O título sugere forte relevância a process mining em contexto de software (ESEM 2011), potencialmente atendendo IC1, mas sem abstract disponível não é possível confirmar se o foco é efetivamente em processos de desenvolvimento de software ou se é BPM genérico. A falta de informação torna a avaliação incerta.
ft_rationale: O artigo trata de process mining em domínio genérico de negócios (business process mining / BPM), focando em avaliação de efetividade da técnica MARBLE através de métricas (recall/precision). Não há evidência no resumo de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software, artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou modelagem estocástica. Atende EC1 (domínio fora de SW) — trata negócio genérico sem link específico a engenharia de software.

title: On the use of patterns to recover business processes
abstract: Legacy systems keep key business knowledge from companies over time. This knowledge is hidden in the source code lines and must be recovered through software archeology processes to maintain and help the legacy systems to evolve, so that the ROI and lifespan of those systems can be improved. This paper proposes a set of patterns to obtain, in a deterministic manner, business models from the source code of legacy systems. Thus, the business process models recovered from the legacy systems preserve the business knowledge and can be used to modernize and maintain the legacy systems.
venue: Proceedings of the ACM Symposium on Applied Computing
ta_rationale: O título sugere recuperação de processos de negócio usando padrões, potencialmente relevante para process mining (IC1), mas sem abstract disponível não é possível confirmar se se aplica a processos de software ou a domínios genéricos de BPM. A venue (ACM SAC 2010) é credível, mas o escopo permanece ambíguo.
ft_rationale: O paper propõe padrões para recuperar modelos de negócio a partir de código-fonte legado, mas não aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting de métricas de processo ou mineração de repositórios. Trata-se de engenharia reversa genérica de sistemas legados (software como ferramenta/artefato, não processo de desenvolvimento), sem análise de event logs, descoberta de processos ou predição temporal. Não atende a nenhum IC.

title: Ontological description and Similarity-Based discovery of business process models
abstract: Project repositories are a central asset in software development, as they preserve the knowledge gathered in past development activities. Locating relevant information in a vast project repository is problematic, because it requires manually tagging projects with accurate metadata, an activity which is time consuming and prone to errors and omissions. Just like any other artifact or web service, business processes can be stored in repositories to be shared and used by third parties, e.g., as building blocks for constructing new business processes. The success of such a paradigm depends partly on the availability of effective search tools to locate business processes that are relevant to the user purposes. A handful of researchers have investigated the problem of business process discovery using as input syntactical and structural information that describes business processes. This work explores an additional source of information encoded in the form of annotations that semantically describe business processes. Business processes can be semantically described using the so called abstract business processes. These are designated by concepts from an ontology which additionally captures their relationships. This ontology can be built in an automatic fashion from a collection of (concrete) business processes, and this work illustrates how it can be refined by domain experts and used in the discovery of business processes, with the purpose of reuse and increase in design productivity.
venue: Frameworks for Developing Efficient Information Systems Models Theory and Practice
ta_rationale: Título sugere foco em descoberta de modelos de processo (potencialmente relacionado a IC1), mas sem abstract disponível não é possível confirmar se aborda especificamente processos de software ou modelagem estocástica. Pode ser BPM genérico fora do escopo de SW.
ft_rationale: O resumo descreve descoberta de modelos de processos de negócios (BPM) genéricos usando ontologias e similaridade semântica, sem qualquer menção a processos de desenvolvimento de software, artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD), event logs de software, ou modelagem estocástica. O domínio abordado é BPM genérico, não engenharia de software. Atende EC1 (domínio fora de SW) e nenhum IC é claramente atendido.

Locating relevant information in a vast project repository is problematic, because it requires manually tagging projects with accurate metadata, an activity which is time consuming and prone to errors and omissions. Just like any other artifact or web service, business processes can be stored in repositories to be shared and used by third parties, e.g., as building blocks for constructing new business processes. The success of such a paradigm depends partly on the availability of effective search tools to locate business processes that are relevant to the user purposes. A handful of researchers have investigated the problem of business process discovery using as input syntactical and structural information that describes business processes. This work explores an additional source of information encoded in the form of annotations that semantically describe business processes. Business processes can be semantically described using the so called abstract business processes. These are designated by concepts from an ontology which additionally captures their relationships. This ontology can be built in an automatic fashion from a collection of (concrete) business processes, and this work illustrates how it can be refined by domain experts and used in the discovery of business processes, with the purpose of reuse and increase in design productivity.
venue: International Journal of Information System Modeling and Design
ta_rationale: O título sugere descoberta de modelos de processo de negócio com base em ontologia e similaridade, potencialmente relevante para IC4 (mineração de repositórios/descoberta de processos). Porém, sem abstract disponível, não é possível confirmar se aplica-se especificamente a processos de software ou se é genérico de BPM. A data (2011) e venue genérica aumentam incerteza sobre escopo de SW.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda descoberta e reuso de modelos de processos de negócio (BPM) genéricos em repositórios usando ontologia e similaridade semântica. Não há evidência no resumo de aplicação específica a processos de desenvolvimento de software (commits, issues, CI/CD, VCS). A menção a 'project repositories' refere-se a repositórios de processos de negócio abstratos, não a artefatos de software ou event logs de desenvolvimento. Assim, EC1 (domínio fora de SW) e ausência de qualquer IC claramente aplicá

title: Optimal and adaptive testing with cost constraints
abstract: This paper generalizes our previous work on optimal and adaptive testing to consider a more general scenario of software testing resource constraints. The assumption is that software testing must be stopped once the allowed testing resources are used up. The contributions of this paper are as follows. First, we show that software testing with fixed resource constraints can be handled in the framework of the controlled Markov chains (CMC) approach to software testing. Second, an algorithm is adopted to reduce the computational complexity of on-line decision making in optimal and adaptive testing. Finally, the simulation results presented in this paper further confirm the effectiveness of the idea of adaptive testing in particular, and that of software cybernetics (which explores the interplay between software and control) in general.
venue: Proceedings International Conference on Software Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere otimização de testes em software, potencialmente relevante a processos de desenvolvimento, mas sem abstract disponível não é possível confirmar se trata process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting de métricas de processo, ou mineração de repositórios conforme escopo PATHCAST.
ft_rationale: O paper trata otimização de testes de software via cadeias de Markov controladas (CMC), mas foca em decisões de parada de testes sob restrições de recursos — não aborda process mining (descoberta/análise de event logs), modelagem estocástica de processos de desenvolvimento, forecasting de métricas de processo, ou mineração de repositórios. A modelagem estocástica presente não está alinhada ao escopo PATHCAST (que requer Markov/Monte Carlo aplicado a *processos de desenvolvimento*, não apenas tes

title: Optimal scheduling of software projects using reinforcement learning
abstract: We compute optimal scheduling policies for software development projects. We use reinforcement learning as the optimization technique. Our approach is based on a formal, stochastic scheduling model that explicitly captures the strong feedback between the tasks in software development ("ripple effects"). For sample projects, we compute the optimal policy, simulate the project, and analyze the task assignments that are made by the optimal policy. We find that optimal policies typically assign tasks according to the past performance of the developers and the characteristics of the software design. In particular, we address the problem of when to schedule large or strongly coupled components. We also sketch approaches to the optimization of large projects.
venue: Proceedings Asia Pacific Software Engineering Conference APSEC
ta_rationale: Título sugere otimização de cronograma em projetos de SW, potencialmente relevante para IC3 (forecasting/predição de métricas de processo), mas sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se aplica process mining, modelagem estocástica ou análise de event logs. A abordagem por reinforcement learning é ortogonal aos métodos esperados (PM, modelos estocásticos clássicos).
ft_rationale: O paper trata otimização de cronograma em projetos de SW usando reinforcement learning e modelos estocásticos de escalonamento, mas não atende aos critérios de inclusão da SLR PATHCAST. Não há evidência de (1) aplicação de process mining a artefatos de SW (IC1), (2) uso de técnicas estocásticas clássicas (Markov, Monte Carlo, Petri nets) para modelar processos de desenvolvimento (IC2), (3) forecasting de métricas de processo baseado em event logs (IC3), ou (4) mineração de repositórios para desc

title: Optimal test profile in the context of software cybernetics
abstract: Software cybernetics explores the interplay between software theory/engineering and control theory/engineering Following the idea of software cybernetics, the controlled Markov chains (CMC) approach to software testing treats software testing as a control problem. The software under test serves as a controlled object, and the (optimal) testing strategy determined by the theory of controlled Markov chains serves as a controller. The software under test and the corresponding (optimal) testing strategy constitute a closed-loop feedback system, and the software state transitions behave as a Markov chain. The paper analyzes the behavior of the corresponding optimal test profile determined by the CMC approach to software testing. It is shown that in some cases the optimal test profile is Markovian, whereas in some other cases the optimal test profile demonstrates a different scenario. The analyses presented in the paper deepen our understanding of the CMC approach to software testing and are related to software operational profile modeling.
venue: Proceedings 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Quality Software Apaqs 2001
ta_rationale: Título sugere contexto de teste de software, potencialmente relacionado a processos de SW, mas sem resumo disponível é impossível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de métricas de processo conforme escopo PATHCAST. A terminologia 'software cybernetics' é ambígua.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda modelagem estocástica (Markov chains) em contexto de testing de software, o que poderia sugerir IC2. Contudo, o foco é em estratégias de teste otimizadas via control theory e operational profiles, não em descoberta/análise de processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC1), forecasting de métricas de processo de desenvolvimento (IC3), ou mineração de repositórios (IC4). O escopo PATHCAST requer intersecção entre process mining/estocástico E processos de DESENVOLVIMENTO de software;

title: Perturbation analysis of stochastic systems with empirical distribution parameters
abstract: Probabilistic model checking is a quantitative verification technology for computer systems and has been the focus of intense research for over a decade. While in many circumstances of probabilistic model checking it is reasonable to anticipate a possible discrepancy between a stochastic model and a real-world system it represents, the state-of-the-art provides little account for the effects of this discrepancy on verification results. To address this problem, we present a perturbation approach in which quantities such as transition probabilities in the stochastic model are allowed to be perturbed from their measured values. We present a rigorous mathematical characterization for variations that can occur to verification results in the presence of model perturbations. The formal treatment is based on the analysis of a parametric variant of discrete-time Markov chains, called parametric Markov chains (PMCs), which are equipped with a metric to measure their perturbed vector variables. We employ an asymptotic method from perturbation theory to compute two forms of perturbation bounds, namely condition numbers and quadratic bounds, for automata-based verification of PMCs. We also evaluate our approach with case studies on variant models for three widely studied systems, the Zeroconf protocol, the Leader Election Protocol and the NAND Multiplexer.
venue: Proceedings International Conference on Software Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere análise estocástica (potencialmente relevante para IC2), mas sem resumo disponível é impossível confirmar se aplica a processos de software ou se trata apenas de sistema genérico. Venue é ICSE (relevante), porém falta contexto para descartar EC3 (método teórico sem aplicação a SW).
ft_rationale: O paper trata de verificação probabilística e análise de perturbação em cadeias de Markov, mas os case studies (Zeroconf, Leader Election, NAND Multiplexer) são protocolos e sistemas genéricos, não processos de desenvolvimento de software. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento. Enquadra-se em EC3: método teórico (PMC, perturbation bounds) sem avaliação em contexto de processos de SW.

title: Practical large scale what-if queries: Case studies with software risk assessment
abstract: When a lack of data inhibits decision-making, large-scale what-if queries can be conducted over the uncertain parameter ranges. Such queries can generate an overwhelming amount of data. We describe a general method for understanding that data. Large-scale what-if queries can guide Monte Carlo simulations of a model. Machine learning can then be used to summarize the output. The summarization is an ensemble of decision trees. The TARZAN system [so-called because it swings through (or searches) the decision trees] can poll the ensemble looking for majority conclusions regarding what factors change the classifications of the data. TARZAN can succinctly present the results from very large what-if queries. For example, in one of the studies presented, we can view the significant features from 10/sup 9/ what-if queries on half a page.
venue: Proceedings Ase 2000 15th IEEE International Conference on Automated Software Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere análise de cenários (what-if queries) em risco de software, potencialmente relacionado a modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de processos de SW, mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica de processos de desenvolvimento, ou forecasting de métricas de processo conforme escopo PATHCAST.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda modelagem de incerteza e simulação Monte Carlo para análise de risco de software, mas não trata de process mining, modelagem estocástica de processos de desenvolvimento de SW, forecasting de métricas de processo, ou mineração de repositórios. A aplicação é genérica (risco de software) sem vinculação a artefatos de desenvolvimento (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou event logs. EC3 aplica-se: algoritmo teórico (decision trees, TARZAN) sem avaliação em contexto de processo de SW.

title: Predicting data access patterns in object-oriented applications based on Markov chains
abstract: This work aims to create an innovative system for analyzing and predicting the behaviour of object-oriented applications, with respect to the domain objects they manipulate, based on Markov Chains. The results are validated by the execution of the TPC-W and oo7 benchmarks. The oo7 benchmark has been modelled as a stochastic process through Monte Carlo simulations. The system is sufficiently flexible to be applied to a broad spectrum of object-oriented applications. The results are precise, regarding the observed behaviour, and the overheads introduced by the data acquisition are low.
venue: Proceedings 5th International Conference on Software Engineering Advances Icsea 2010
ta_rationale: Título menciona Markov chains (potencial IC2) aplicado a aplicações orientadas a objetos, mas sem abstract não é claro se aborda processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou apenas padrões de acesso em tempo de execução. Risco de ser EC2 (SW como ferramenta/implementação, não processo).
ft_rationale: O paper aborda predição de padrões de acesso a dados em aplicações orientadas a objetos usando Markov chains e Monte Carlo, focando em comportamento em tempo de execução e desempenho. Não há evidência de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC1, IC3, IC4 não se aplicam). A modelagem estocástica (IC2) é usada para prever padrões de acesso de dados, não para modelar ou melhorar processos de desenvolvimento. Enquadra-se em EC2: software (aplicações OO) é a ferramenta/sistema sendo

title: Predicting release time based on Generalized Software Reliability Model (GSRM)
abstract: Development environments have changed drastically, development periods are shorter than ever and the number of team members has increased. Especially in open source software (OSS), a large number of developers contribute to OSS. OSS has difficulties in predicting or deciding when it will be released. In order to assess recent software developments, we proposed a generalized software reliability model (GSRM) based on a stochastic process, and compared GSRM with other models. In this paper, we focus on the release dates of OSS and the growth of faults (issues).
venue: Proceedings International Computer Software and Applications Conference
ta_rationale: O título sugere forecasting de release time usando modelo de confiabilidade de software (GSRM), potencialmente alinhado com IC3. Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se o estudo efetivamente minera event logs ou repositórios de processos de SW, ou se aplica modelagem estocástica a processos de desenvolvimento. A ausência de abstract impede avaliação precisa contra critérios de inclusão e exclusão.
ft_rationale: O paper propõe um modelo estocástico generalizado (GSRM) baseado em processo estocástico para prever datas de release e crescimento de falhas (issues) em OSS. Atende IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW) e IC3 (forecasting de métricas de processo, especificamente release time). O uso de modelo de confiabilidade estocástico aplicado a repositórios OSS (minera dados de issues/releases) alinha-se ao escopo PATHCAST.

title: Predicting time range of development based on generalized software reliability model
abstract: Development environments have changed drastically, development periods are shorter than ever and the number of team members has increased. These changes have led to difficulties in controlling the development activities and predicting when the development will end. Especially, quality managers try to control software reliability and project managers try to estimate the end of development for planning developing term and distribute the manpower to other developments. In order to assess recent software developments, we propose a generalized software reliability model (GSRM) based on a stochastic process, and simulate developments that include uncertainties and dynamics. We also compare our simulation results to those of other software reliability models. Using the values of uncertainties and dynamics obtained from GSRM, we can evaluate the developments in a quantitative manner. Additionally, we use equations to define the uncertainty regarding the time required to complete a development, and predict whether or not a development will be completed on time. We compare GSRM with an existing model using two old actual datasets and one new actual dataset which we collected, and show that the approximation curve generated by GSRM is about 12% more precise than that generated by the existing model. Furthermore, GSRM can narrow down the predicted time range in which a development will end to less than 40% of that obtained by the existing model.
venue: Proceedings Asia Pacific Software Engineering Conference APSEC
ta_rationale: O título sugere forecasting de tempo de desenvolvimento (potencialmente IC3), mas sem resumo disponível é impossível confirmar se usa event logs, process mining, ou modelagem estocástica formal. A referência a 'software reliability model' pode indicar abordagem estocástica relevante, mas falta clareza sobre aplicação a processos de SW versus métricas de qualidade isoladas.
ft_rationale: O paper propõe um modelo estocástico de confiabilidade de software (GSRM) para prever o tempo de conclusão do desenvolvimento, mas não atende aos critérios de inclusão. Não há evidência de process mining (IC1), análise de event logs de processos de SW, ou aplicação de modelagem estocástica explicitamente ligada a processos de desenvolvimento (IC2). O trabalho foca em forecasting de métrica isolada (tempo de conclusão) usando modelo de confiabilidade tradicional, sem mineração de repositórios ou

title: Probabilistic analysis of process economics
abstract: Abstract The economic benefit of a certain development process or particular activity is usually unknown and indeed hard to predict. However, the cost‐effectiveness of process improvements is of paramount importance and the question how profitable certain activities are needs to be answered. Within a large‐scale commercial organization, we were challenged with the task to quantify the economic benefit of isolated test and development environments. To answer this question, we defined a generic process model based on absorbing Markov chains that allows the analyzation of the economic benefit of software process variations. This model exposes conflicts between process steps and reiterations of development activities and thereby provides a highly flexible tool for the investigation of the effects of changes to a development process on its overall performance. This model was used to predict the impact of isolated testing on the overall effort and duration of projects. The results obtained correspond well with the perception of experienced developers and gives a detailed explanation for the effects. Besides this, it can be used to analyze various other economic aspects of software development processes and yields an interesting alternative for cost estimation. Copyright © 2008 John Wiley & Sons, Ltd.
venue: Software Process Improvement and Practice
ta_rationale: Título sugere análise probabilística/estocástica em economia de processos, potencialmente relevante para IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software específicos e não de processos econômicos genéricos.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica explicitamente cadeias de Markov absorventes (modelagem estocástica) para analisar processos de desenvolvimento de software, permitindo prever impacto de variações nos processos sobre esforço e duração de projetos. Atende IC2 (modelagem estocástica em processos de SW) com clareza, sem incidência de EC.

title: Probabilistic petri net and its logical semantics
abstract: There are many variants of Petri net at present, and some of them can model system with both function and performance specification, such as stochastic Petri net, and generalized stochastic Petri net. In order to address the issue of modeling system with probabilistic behaviors, a kind of Petri net with probability (probabilistic Petri net, PPN) is proposed in this paper. Then an action-based PCTL is developed to interpret logical semantics for PPN system. The usefulness of PPN system is illustrated by modeling and specifying an elaborate model of travel arrangements workflow.
venue: Proceedings 2011 9th International Conference on Software Engineering Research Management and Applications SERA 2011
ta_rationale: Título sugere relevância potencial para IC2 (modelagem estocástica com Petri nets), mas ausência de resumo impede avaliação da aplicação específica a processos de software. Pode ser método teórico puro (EC3) ou aplicado a domínio fora de SW (EC1).
ft_rationale: O artigo propõe uma extensão teórica de Petri nets probabilísticas (PPN) com semântica lógica PCTL, ilustrada apenas com um exemplo de workflow de arranjos de viagem genérico. Não há evidência de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software (commits, issues, CI/CD, repositórios) nem de mineração de dados de SW. Atende EC3 (algoritmo/método teórico sem avaliação em processos de SW) e EC1 (domínio é workflow genérico, não SW).

title: Process conformance using CSP
abstract: One of the frequently occurring problems in process aware information systems is to determine if the observations (in the form of events logs) match/fit the behavior of the reference system (modeled as a business process). This problem is more commonly known as "process conformance checking". In this paper we tackle this problem using concepts from Communicating Sequential Processes (CSP), which facilitates automated analysis using FDR model checker.
venue: Proceedings of the 5th India Software Engineering Conference Isec 12
ta_rationale: Título sugere process conformance (indicador de IC1 - process mining), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de aplicação a processos de SW ou presença de avaliação empírica. Venue de SE (ISEC) é favorável, mas insuficiente sem conteúdo.
ft_rationale: O paper trata explicitamente de process conformance checking usando event logs (observações) comparadas contra um modelo de referência (business process). Isso se alinha claramente com IC1 (process mining — conformance checking é uma atividade central de process mining). O contexto é sistemas de informação conscientes de processos em engenharia de software (ISEC 2012), e o método usa CSP/FDR para análise automatizada, indicando avaliação técnica. Nenhum EC se aplica decisivamente.

title: Process discovery: Automated approach for block discovery
abstract: Process mining is a set of techniques helping enterprises to avoid process modeling which is a time-consuming and error prone task. Process mining includes three topics: process discovery, conformance checking, and enhancement (IEEE Task Force on Process Mining: Process Mining Manifesto, 2012). The principle of process discovery is to extract information from event logs to capture the business process as it is being executed. Several techniques in literature (a algorithm, a+ algorithm and others) can be applied to discover a process model from a workflow log. However, as the amount of information grows exponentially, the log files (input of a process discovery algorithm) get bigger. In fact, classical techniques, which inspect relation between each couple of tasks will have problem dealing with big data. To this end, we introduced in (Boushaba et al., 2013) a new approach aiming to extract a block of tasks from event logs. In this paper, we present a new algorithm, based on a matrix representation, to detect a block of tasks. In addition, we develop an application to automate our technique.
venue: Enase 2014 Proceedings of the 9th International Conference on Evaluation of Novel Approaches to Software Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere process discovery (potencialmente IC1), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação do domínio de aplicação e se realmente trata de processos de SW ou BPM genérico. A venue (ENASE) é relevante para SE, mas sem abstract não é possível descartar que seja puramente teórico ou aplicado a domínio não-SW.
ft_rationale: O paper apresenta uma técnica genérica de process discovery (detecção de blocos de tarefas) aplicada a event logs, mas não há evidência de aplicação específica a processos de desenvolvimento de software. O resumo discute BPM clássico e escalabilidade de algoritmos sem vincular a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, CI/CD) ou processos de desenvolvimento. Assim, EC1 (domínio fora de SW) se aplica—é BPM genérico, não PM em SE.

title: Process mining and verification of properties: An approach based on temporal logic
abstract: Information systems are facing conflicting requirements. On the one hand, systems need to be adaptive and self-managing to deal with rapidly changing circumstances. On the other hand, legislation such as the Sarbanes-Oxley Act, is putting increasing demands on monitoring activities and processes. As processes and systems become more flexible, both the need for, and the complexity of monitoring increases. Our earlier work on process mining has primarily focused on process discovery, i.e., automatically constructing models describing knowledge extracted from event logs. In this paper, we focus on a different problem complementing process discovery. Given an event log and some property, we want to verify whether the property holds. For this purpose we have developed a new language based on Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) and we combine this with a standard XML format to store event logs. Given an event log and an LTL property, our LTL Checker verifies whether the observed behavior matches the (un)expected/(un)desirable behavior
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: Título indica process mining com foco em verificação formal via lógica temporal, potencialmente relevante para IC1 (process mining), mas sem abstract disponível para confirmar aplicação a processos de SW versus BPM genérico. A ausência de resumo impede avaliação clara do domínio de aplicação e se atende critérios de inclusão.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda process mining com foco em verificação de propriedades via Linear Temporal Logic (LTL), mas não atende aos critérios de inclusão da SLR PATHCAST. Não há evidência de (1) aplicação a artefatos de SW específicos (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD), (2) modelagem estocástica, (3) forecasting de métricas de processo, ou (4) mineração de repositórios de software. O contexto é BPM genérico (conformance checking via LTL), não desenvolvimento de software.

title: Process mining: software comparison, trends, and challenges
venue: International Journal of Data Science and Analytics
ta_rationale: Título fortemente sugestivo de escopo relevante (process mining em software), mas ausência de resumo impede avaliação conclusiva de conteúdo, cobertura de aplicações práticas em SW, e atendimento aos critérios de inclusão específicos.
ft_rationale: Título fortemente sugere relevância ao escopo (process mining em software), mas ausência de abstract impossibilita avaliação conclusiva do conteúdo, metodologia, e atendimento aos critérios de inclusão específicos (IC1-IC4). Classificado como review/survey em 2023 — requer acesso ao texto completo para determinar se trata efetivamente de process mining aplicado a SW ou constitui revisão secundária fora do escopo (EC4). Excluído por inacessibilidade de texto completo (sem PDF/OA disponível).

title: Qualitative modeling for requirements engineering
abstract: Acquisition of "quantitative" models of sufficient accuracy to enable effective analysis of requirements tradeoffs is hampered by the slowness and difficulty of obtaining sufficient data. "Qualitative" models, based on expert opinion, can be built quickly and therefore used earlier. Such qualitative models are nondeterminate which makes them hard to use for making categorical policy decisions over the model. The nondeterminacy of qualitative models can be tamed using "stochastic sampling" and "treatment learning". These tools can quickly find and set the "master variables" that restrain qualitative simulations. Once tamed, qualitative modeling can be used in requirements engineering to assess more options, earlier in the life cycle
venue: Proceedings of the 30th Annual IEEE NASA Software Engineering Workshop Sew 30
ta_rationale: Título sugere foco em engenharia de requisitos (RE) e modelagem qualitativa, potencialmente relevante a processos de SW, mas sem resumo não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting ou mineração de repositórios conforme escopo PATHCAST.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de modelagem qualitativa e estocástica para engenharia de requisitos, não abordando process mining, modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento de SW, forecasting de métricas de processo ou mineração de repositórios. Aplica-se EC3: método teórico (stochastic sampling, treatment learning) sem avaliação em processos de SW; e EC2: software é ferramenta/domínio de aplicação, não o processo de desenvolvimento sendo estudado.

title: Quantifying failure risk of version switch for rolling upgrade on clouds
abstract: Rolling upgrade is an industry technique for online dynamic software update. A rolling upgrade updates a small number of instances in an old version to a new version at a time and the operation is repeated in a wave rolling until all of the instances have been upgraded. In many cases, the software needs to avoid interactions between different versions. One common simple approach is to make instances version aware, and then a version switch point can be chosen to deactivate the old service and activate the new service. On a Cloud platform, upgrades can be implemented simply through replacing old virtual machine instances with ones in new versions, and during the process of rolling upgrade various failures may present. If an instance fails, a new instance has to be launched from the backup images, which in most software systems are in an old version and cannot be simply replaced to a new version if the new software and the new service have not been stable for the sake of reliability and stability. Thus the progress of the rolling upgrade is not guaranteed, and indeed the number of upgraded instances can sometimes decrease. We aim to determine the probability that, after switching the versions at a selected point, the number of working instances may sometime fall below the amount needed for a desired Quality of Service. In this paper, we stochastically quantify the risk with a family of discrete Markov chains (DTMC). The evaluation in both Amazon Web Service (AWS) and simulation reveals that our technique can well predict the risks after given version switch points.
venue: Proceedings 4th IEEE International Conference on Big Data and Cloud Computing Bdcloud 2014 with the 7th IEEE International Conference on Social Computing and Networking Socialcom 2014 and the 4th International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Communications Sustaincom 2014
ta_rationale: Título sugere análise de risco em processos de atualização de software na nuvem, potencialmente relevante para IC3 (forecasting de falhas/risco). Porém, sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se trata efetivamente de modelagem estocástica, process mining ou event log analysis em processos de SW, ou se foca em infraestrutura/cloud computing como domínio principal (EC1).
ft_rationale: O paper foca em modelagem estocástica (DTMC) de risco de falha em rolling upgrades na nuvem, mas não aborda process mining, event log analysis ou forecasting de métricas de processo de desenvolvimento de software (IC1-IC4 não se aplicam). Embora use Markov chains (componente estocástica), aplica-se a infraestrutura/operações de cloud computing e deployment, não a artefatos ou processos de SW development (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD). A análise é de confiabilidade de sistema em produção, não de pa

title: Quantifying software test process and product reliability simultaneously
abstract: Software reliability models (SRMs) are used to assess software reliability and to control quantitatively software testing. In this paper we consider metrics-based SRMs and tackle a statistical estimation of both software test process and product reliability simultaneously. The basic idea is to apply the Markov-dependent Poisson regression to describe the random testing environment by a discrete-time Markov chain. We formulate four Markov-dependent Poisson regression-based SRMs and develop the EM (expectation-maximization) algorithms to estimate the maximum likelihood estimates of model parameters. Numerical examples with real software project data show that our approach is useful to quantify both of software test process and software product reliability, and can answer the question why the stability of test process can lead to the improvement of software product reliability.
venue: 2013 IEEE 24th International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering ISSRE 2013
ta_rationale: Título sugere análise de processo de teste de software e confiabilidade, potencialmente relevante para IC3 (forecasting de métricas de processo). Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting explicitamente, ou se limita-se a métodos tradicionais de engenharia de confiabilidade de software.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda modelagem estocástica (Markov-dependent Poisson regression) aplicada a confiabilidade de software, mas NÃO se enquadra nos critérios de inclusão da SLR PATHCAST. Não há aplicação de process mining (IC1), não modela processos de desenvolvimento de SW com Markov (IC2 requer SW dev process, não test process reliability), não faz forecasting de métricas de processo (IC3 — lead time, cycle time, throughput em dev; aqui é confiabilidade de produto), e não minera repositórios (IC4). O fo

title: Quantifying the variance in application reliability
abstract: A notable drawback of the existing architecture-based reliability assessment techniques is that they only obtain a point estimate of application reliability and do not attempt to quantify the variance in the estimate. The variance in the reliability estimate of an application represents the risk associated with the estimate. Ideally, the variance should be zero, but in practice it is inevitable due to the following two factors: (i) variances in the reliability estimates of components comprising the application, and (ii) architectural characteristics of the application. Quantifying the variance in the reliability estimate of an application provides an indication of the degree of risk associated with the estimate, and can also suggest an appropriate variance reduction strategy. We present a technique to quantify the variance in the reliability estimate of an application based on its architecture. Our technique generates analytical functions which express the mean and variance of application reliability in terms of the means and variances of the component reliabilities as well as the architectural characteristics of the application. Through a case study, we illustrate how the analytical functions generated using our technique could be used to evaluate the impact of individual components on the mean and the variance in the application reliability.
venue: Proceedings IEEE Pacific Rim International Symposium on Dependable Computing
ta_rationale: Título sugere foco em confiabilidade de aplicações, potencialmente relevante a processos de SW, mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de métricas de processo de desenvolvimento. Pode ser estudo de confiabilidade de produto (fora do escopo) ou de processo (dentro do escopo).
ft_rationale: O paper trata de técnicas de avaliação de confiabilidade de arquitetura de aplicações (product reliability assessment), focando em quantificar variância em estimativas de confiabilidade de componentes e sistemas. Não aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica de processos de desenvolvimento de software, forecasting de métricas de processo de SW, ou mineração de repositórios para descoberta de processos. Aplica-se EC1 (domínio fora de SW — confiabilidade de produto/componentes) e nenhum IC é at

title: Quantitative verification: Models, techniques and tools
abstract: Automated verification is a technique for establishing if certain properties, usually expressed in temporal logic, hold for a system model. The model can be defined using a high-level formalism or extracted directly from software using methods such as abstract interpretation. The verification proceeds through exhaustive exploration of the state-transition graph of the model and is therefore more powerful than testing. Quantitative verification is an analogous technique for establishing quantitative properties of a system model, such as the probability of battery power dropping below minimum, the expected time for message delivery and the expected number of messages lost before protocol termination. Models analysed through this method are typically variants of Markov chains, annotated with costs and rewards that describe resources and their usage during execution. Properties are expressed in temporal logic extended with probabilistic and reward operators. Quantitative verification involves a combination of a traversal of the state-transition graph of the model and numerical computation.
venue: Esec Fse 07 6th Joint Meeting of the European Software Engineering Conference and the ACM SIGSOFT Symposium on the Foundations of Software Engineering Companion Papers
ta_rationale: Título sugere potencial relevância a modelagem estocástica/verificação quantitativa, mas sem abstract não é possível confirmar aplicação a processos de SW ou event logs. Pode ser trabalho teórico ou aplicado a domínios diversos.
ft_rationale: O artigo trata de verificação quantitativa e Markov chains como técnica geral de modelagem de sistemas de software, não especificamente de process mining, event logs ou modelagem de processos de desenvolvimento de software. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) ou descoberta de modelos de processo a partir de dados. Enquadra-se em EC3 — método teórico sem avaliação específica em processos de SW.

title: Reconstructing meaningful workflow from transaction logs
abstract: Enterprises today rely on workflow more and more to accomplish business goals. A well designed process can contribute to better business operation and working efficiency. In practice, however, most organizations have no efficient workflow, either there is not at all from the very beginning, or lack domain experts who equip with the comprehensive landscape about the business. In order to conduct business more effectively, it is necessary to reconstruct workflows from transaction logs, and further, optimize the business via meaningful representations of these workflows. In this paper, we propose a method and corresponding toolbox to mine the workflows and vital patterns. Domain experts can use them in an interactive way to reconstruct meaningful business processes. We got very promising experimental results on two real applications datasets.
venue: Proceedings 2010 IEEE International Conference on Software Engineering and Service Sciences Icsess 2010
ta_rationale: Título sugere workflow mining de transaction logs, potencialmente relevante para IC1 (process mining em software), mas sem resumo disponível não é possível confirmar se aplica-se a processos de SW ou a outro domínio (BPM genérico, saúde, finanças). A data (2010) e venue (ICSESS) indicam contexto de engenharia de software, mas a falta de abstract impede avaliação definitiva.
ft_rationale: O resumo descreve workflow mining de transaction logs em contexto empresarial genérico ('enterprises', 'business processes'), sem evidência explícita de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC1 exigiria commits, issues, PR, CI/CD, ou artefatos de SW). O texto refere-se a 'business workflow' e 'business operation', sugerindo BPM genérico ou processos corporativos, não SE. Atende EC1 (domínio fora de SW) ou falha em atender qualquer IC com confiança.

title: Reliability estimation and prediction of SA producing process based on Markov chain
abstract: SA-based software reliability estimation, which is applied in the early stage of software life cycle, is used to allocate resources to components to achieve the desired reliability effectively. This paper proposed an approach to predict reliability of the whole SA producing process by estimating and evaluating the different stages' reliability in SA producing process based on Markov chain. A case study illustrates the applicability of the approach.
venue: Iccasm 2010 2010 International Conference on Computer Application and System Modeling Proceedings
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (Markov chain) aplicada a um processo, potencialmente alinhado com IC2, mas a ausência de resumo impede confirmação clara do domínio (SA = Software Architecture? System Application?), se é processo de SW ou outro domínio, e se há efetiva aplicação prática em engenharia de software.
ft_rationale: O resumo refere-se a 'SA producing process' (processo de produção de SA, interpretado como Software Architecture ou System Application) e propõe estimação de confiabilidade usando Markov chain. Contudo, não há evidência clara de que: (1) aplica process mining (discovery, conformance, workflow mining, event log analysis) — apenas usa Markov chain para modelagem; (2) minera repositórios ou artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD); (3) faz forecasting de métricas de processo (lead time, cycle t

title: Reliability forecasting in complex hardware/software systems
abstract: We describe a class of models used to evaluate and forecast the reliability of complex hardware/software systems. We assume the system may be described through a reliability block diagram. Blocks referring to hardware components are dealt with 'pending' continuous time Markov chain models, whereas blocks referring to software components are dealt with 'pending' software reliability growth models. Inference and forecasting tasks with such models are described.
venue: Proceedings First International Conference on Availability Reliability and Security Ares 2006
ta_rationale: O título sugere forecasting de confiabilidade em sistemas complexos, potencialmente relevante para IC3 (previsão de métricas). Porém, sem abstract disponível, não é possível confirmar se o foco é em processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou em confiabilidade de sistemas em execução. A data (2006) e venue (ARES) indicam foco em segurança/confiabilidade, mas a aplicação a SW development processes permanece incerta.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de forecasting de confiabilidade (reliability) em sistemas hardware/software em operação, usando Markov chains e software reliability growth models. Não há evidência de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD) conforme IC1, nem de modelagem estocástica de processos de desenvolvimento conforme IC2. IC3 (forecasting de métricas de processo) não se aplica, pois o foco é confiabilidade/disponibilidade do sistema entregue, não métricas de process

title: Reliability modeling and assessment for open source cloud software: A stochastic approach
abstract: Software development based on the Open Source Software (OSS) model is being increasingly accepted to stand up servers and applications. In particular, Cloud OSS is now attracting attention as the next generation of software products due to cost efficiencies and quick delivery. This chapter focuses on the software reliability modeling and assessment for Cloud computing infrastructure software, especially open source software, such as OpenStack and Eucalyptus. In this chapter, the authors introduce a new approach to the Jump diffusion process based on stochastic differential equations in order to consider the interesting aspect of the numbers of components and users in the reliability model. In addition, the authors consider the network traffic of the Cloud in the reliability modeling and integrate the reliability model with a threshold-based neural network approach that estimates network traffic. Actual software fault-count data are analyzed in order to show numerical examples of software reliability assessment. This chapter also illustrates how the proposed method of reliability analysis can assist in quality improvement in Cloud computing software.
venue: Open Source Technology Concepts Methodologies Tools and Applications
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (IC2) em software, mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de que a abordagem estocástica se aplica a processos de desenvolvimento de SW (não apenas confiabilidade de artefatos). Possível EC2 (software como ferramenta/sistema, não processo) sem informação suficiente para decidir.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica modelagem estocástica (jump diffusion processes, SDEs) a software de nuvem, mas o foco é em reliability modeling e assessment de artefatos/sistemas, não em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Não há evidência de process mining, descoberta de processos de desenvolvimento, ou análise de event logs de processos de SW (CI/CD, commits, issues). title: Reliability of task execution during safe software processing
abstract: This paper presents the reliability evaluation of task execution during safe software processing. The standard method of duplication in a safety-critical application can also be applied for tasks in a software system. But in addition to this, there is also the possibility for coded task processing to increase the reliability and availability of software. The presented analysis covers the reliability analysis of a single, a duplicated and a coded task by the technique of continuous time Markov processes. Markov processes are often used for the reliability evaluation of safety-critical systems. We introduce a method to describe the execution time of tasks by means of enhanced Markov models and their solution by numerical methods.
venue: Proceedings 15th Euromicro Conference on Digital System Design Dsd 2012
ta_rationale: Título sugere foco em confiabilidade de execução de tarefas em processamento de software, potencialmente relevante a processos de SW, mas sem abstract disponível é impossível confirmar se atende aos critérios de inclusão (IC1-IC4) ou se incorre em exclusões (especialmente EC2 - software como ferramenta vs. processo).
ft_rationale: O paper aplica Markov processes para análise de confiabilidade de execução de tarefas em sistemas de software, mas o foco é em propriedades de confiabilidade técnica (duplication, coding) de tarefas isoladas, não em modelagem ou predição de processos de desenvolvimento de software (e.g., lead time, cycle time, workflow mining). Atende potencialmente IC2 (modelagem estocástica), mas com aplicação a execução de tarefas em tempo real, não a processos de desenvolvimento/engenharia de software como e

title: Replicating mining studies with SOFAS
abstract: The replication of studies in mining software repositories (MSR) is essential to compare different mining techniques or assess their findings across many projects. However, it has been shown that very few of these studies can be easily replicated. Their replication is just as fundamental as the studies themselves and is one of the main threats to validity that they suffer from. In this paper, we show how we can alleviate this problem with our SOFAS framework. SOFAS is a platform that enables a systematic and repeatable analysis of software projects by providing extensible and composable analysis workflows. These workflows can be applied on a multitude of software projects, facilitating the replication and scaling of mining studies. In this paper, we show how and to which degree replication can be achieved. We investigated the mining studies of MSR from 2004 to 2011 and found that from 88 studies published in the MSR proceedings so far, we can fully replicate 25 empirical studies. Additionally, we can replicate 27 additional studies to a large extent. These studies account for 30% and 32%, respectively, of the mining studies published. To support our claim we describe in detail one large study that we replicated and discuss how replication with SOFAS works for the other studies investigated. To discuss the potential of our platform we also characterise how studies can be easily enriched to deliver even more comprehensive answers by extending the analysis workflows provided by the platform.
venue: IEEE International Working Conference on Mining Software Repositories
ta_rationale: Título sugere mineração de repositórios de software (MSR) e replicação de estudos, potencialmente relevante para IC4 (mineração de repositórios para processo). Contudo, sem abstract disponível, não é possível confirmar se o foco é em processos de desenvolvimento de software ou se concentra em outras dimensões do mining (ex: defeitos, arquitetura, evolução sem foco em processo).
ft_rationale: O paper apresenta um framework (SOFAS) para replicação de estudos de mineração de repositórios, mas não aplica process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting a processos de desenvolvimento de software. O foco está em replicabilidade de técnicas de MSR genéricas, não em descoberta/análise de processos de desenvolvimento. Não atende nenhum dos ICs de escopo da SLR PATHCAST.

title: Representation and optimization of software usage models with non-Markovian state transitions
venue: Information and Software Technology
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (non-Markovian state transitions) aplicada a software usage models, potencialmente alinhado com IC2. Porém, sem abstract disponível, é impossível confirmar se trata processos de desenvolvimento de SW ou modelos de uso/comportamento de sistemas. A ambiguidade entre 'software como sistema' vs 'processo de SW' impede decisão definitiva.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de modelagem estocástica (non-Markovian) aplicada a usage models de software, focando em testes de software e dependabilidade.
