Título menciona 'data mining for software repositories' (potencial IC4) e 'deep learning', mas sem resumo não é possível verificar se o trabalho efetivamente modela processos de desenvolvimento, minera event logs de SW, ou apenas classifica artefatos de código isoladamente. Excluído por inacessibilidade de texto completo (sem PDF/OA disponível).

title: Decomposing replay problems: A case study
abstract: Abstract. Conformance checking is an important field in the process mining area. In brief, conformance checking provides us with insights how well a given process model matches a given event log. To gain these insights, we typically try to replay the event log on the process model. However, this replay problem may be complex and, as a result, may take considerable time. To ease the complexity of the replay, we can decom-pose the given process model into a collection of (smaller) submodels, and associate a (smaller) sublog to every submodel. Instead of replaying the entire event log on the entire process model, we can then replay the corre-sponding sublog on every submodel, and combine the results. This paper tests this divide-and-conquer approach on a number of process models and event logs while using existing replay techniques. Results show that the decomposed replay may indeed be faster by orders of magnitude, but that success is not guaranteed, as in some cases a smaller model and a smaller log yield a more complex replay problem. 1
venue: Ceur Workshop Proceedings
ta_rationale: Título sugere relevância a process mining (replay problems são conceito central em conformance checking), mas sem resumo disponível é impossível confirmar se aplica a processos de SW ou a outro domínio (BPM genérico, manufatura, etc.). Publicação em workshop CEUR de 2013 é consistente com PM, mas falta contexto.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de conformance checking e técnicas de replay em process mining, mas não atende nenhum dos critérios de inclusão. Não há menção a aplicação em processos de software (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD, VCS), modelagem estocástica de processos de SW, forecasting de métricas de processo, ou mineração de repositórios. O trabalho é um estudo técnico de otimização de algoritmos de conformance em BPM genérico, sem evidência de aplicação ao domínio de engenharia de software. EC1 é implícito: o dom

title: Determining optimal testing times for Markov chain usage models
abstract: Statistical software testing presents two difficulties for the tester: (1) establishing accurate user profiles (i.e. usage probabilities), and (2) incurring lengthy test times. An algorithm, named the frequency count method (FCM), is developed which addresses both difficulties simultaneously. FCM finds usage probabilities within predetermined ranges and concurrently minimizes the amount of testing time. First, FCM randomly generates a large number of matrices for a given Markov chain with constrained usage probabilities. For each one-step transition matrix associated with the given Markov chain usage model, FCM simulates the steps of the chain. FCM flags the usage matrix which requires the minimum expected amount of testing time (assuming no failures) and ensures theoretical and calculated stationary probability values are within some preset precision. Thus, by generating test sequences from the usage probabilities of the flagged matrix, the expected minimum statistical testing time is achieved. This minimum time is optimal with respect to the transition probability ranges and the given execution times. Employing a 5-state usage model with numerical values for the transition probability bounds and code execution times, the FCM algorithm is illustrated and the expected minimum testing time is calculated.
venue: Conference Proceedings IEEE SOUTHEASTCON
ta_rationale: Título sugere potencial relevância a IC2 (modelagem estocástica com Markov chains) em contexto de testing, mas sem resumo não é possível confirmar se o foco é em processos de desenvolvimento de software ou em testing de sistemas de software como domínio genérico. A ausência de abstract impede avaliação definitiva.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de modelagem estocástica (Markov chains) aplicada a testing de software, porém o foco é em otimização de tempo de teste e estabelecimento de perfis de uso, não em processos de desenvolvimento de software (CI/CD, commits, issues, PR) nem em forecasting de métricas de processo. Não há evidência de process mining, discovery de processos de desenvolvimento, ou previsão de lead time/cycle time. O domínio é testing/QA como foco isolado, não a engenharia de processo de desenvolvimento.

title: Event log knowledge as a complementary simulation model construction input
abstract: Business process simulation models are typically built using model construction inputs such as documentation, interviews and observations. Due to issues with these information sources, efforts to further improve the realism of simulation models are valuable. Within this context, the present paper focuses on the use of process execution data in simulation model construction. More specifically, the behaviour of contemporary business processes is increasingly registered in event logs by process-aware information systems. Knowledge can be extracted from these log files using process mining techniques. This paper advocates the addition of event log knowledge as a model construction input, complementary to traditional information sources. A conceptual framework for simulation model construction is presented and the integration of event log knowledge during the modeling of particular simulation model building blocks is outlined. The use of event log knowledge is demonstrated in a simulation of the operations of a roadside assistance company.
venue: Simultech 2014 Proceedings of the 4th International Conference on Simulation and Modeling Methodologies Technologies and Applications
ta_rationale: Título indica potencial relevância para process mining (event logs) e simulação estocástica em processos, alinhado com IC1 e IC2. Porém, sem abstract disponível, é impossível confirmar se o foco é em processos de software ou em domínios genéricos (manufatura, BPM geral), e se atende claramente aos critérios de inclusão PATHCAST.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de simulação de processos de negócio (BPM genérico) usando event logs e process mining, mas o domínio estudado é uma empresa de assistência rodoviária (roadside assistance), não processos de desenvolvimento de software. Não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW (commits, issues, CI/CD) ou processos de desenvolvimento. Aplica-se EC1 — domínio fora de SW, sem link a processos de engenharia de software.

title: Holistic processing and exploring event logs
venue: Lecture Notes in Computer Science Including Subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics
ta_rationale: Título sugere relevância para process mining (event logs), potencialmente atendendo IC1. Contudo, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se aborda processos de SW especificamente ou se aplica a outro domínio (BPM genérico, saúde, manufatura), caracterizando incerteza quanto a EC1.
ft_rationale: Holistic processing de event logs de sistemas gerais, não de processos de desenvolvimento de software.

title: Improving effectiveness of Agile development
abstract: Agile software development has evolved in the past two decades into a set of tools for developing software. It is based on at once models that assume that the creation of software is done by simultaneously working on requirements, analysis, design, coding, and testing, then delivering the entire system all at once. Agile main principle is using an iterative and incremental approach to building software. In Agile practice most of the responsibility is on the hands of the team members that need to estimate the work within iteration and commit to it. In order to accomplish this today, team members use their experience, educated advice, and some rules of thumb. We believe that especially in this stressed environment with almost ?microscopic? work assignments, there should be a simple and fast mathematical model that assists them in this task. The model should handle dynamic environments and risky situations. This paper presents such a model, called HRA, and demonstrate in on a simple but representative real scenario.
venue: Ds 58 1 Proceedings of Iced 09 the 17th International Conference on Engineering Design
ta_rationale: Título sugere foco em Agile development (processo de SW), mas sem resumo não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica, forecasting ou mineração de repositórios. Pode ser estudo genérico de melhoria Agile sem os elementos técnicos do escopo PATHCAST.
ft_rationale: O resumo descreve um modelo matemático (HRA) para estimativa de trabalho em Agile, mas não evidencia aplicação de process mining, modelagem estocástica formal, forecasting de métricas de processo ou mineração de repositórios. Trata-se de proposta genérica de ferramenta de estimativa sem os elementos técnicos centrais do escopo PATHCAST (nenhum IC claramente atendido).

title: Improving event correlation for non-process aware information systems
abstract: Business process mining is a solution to discover business processes. These techniques take event logs recorded by process-aware information systems. Unfortunately, there are many traditional systems without mechanisms for events collection. Techniques for collecting events (which represent the execution of business activities) from non-process-aware systems were proposed to enable the application of process mining to traditional systems. Since business processes supported by traditional systems are implicit, correlating events into their execution instances constitutes a challenge. This paper adapts a previous correlation algorithm and incorporates it into a technique for obtaining event logs from traditional systems. This technique instruments source code to collect events with some additional information. The algorithm is applied to the events dataset to discover the best correlation conditions. Event logs are built using such conditions. The technique is validated with case study, which demonstrates its suitability to discover the correlation set and obtain well-formed event logs.
venue: Enase 2012 Proceedings of the 7th International Conference on Evaluation of Novel Approaches to Software Engineering
ta_rationale: O título sugere relevância a event logs e correlação de eventos em sistemas, potencialmente conectado a process mining. Porém, sem resumo disponível, é impossível confirmar se o foco é em processos de software, se aplica process mining ou modelagem estocástica, ou se 'information systems' refere-se a processos de SW. A venue (ENASE) é relevante para engenharia de software, mas a ambiguidade sobre o domínio de aplicação e a falta de abstract impedem decisão definitiva.
ft_rationale: O paper aplica process mining (descoberta de processos de negócio) a sistemas não process-aware através de coleta e correlação de eventos em logs, adaptando algoritmos para extrair event logs de sistemas tradicionais. Atende IC1 (Process Mining em Software) ao instrumentar código-fonte para coletar eventos e descobrir padrões de execução, com validação em caso de uso prático.

title: Intelligent agile method framework
abstract: Abstract: The paper addresses the problem of the low usage of software development methods in software development practice. This has been recognized as one of the key reasons for failures in software development projects and a contributor to the low quality of software. We introduce a novel approach that could help to improve the maturity of software development processes. The approach is based on the method engineering principles taking into account the limitations that hinder its use in practice. The main objective of our research is to show that the method engineering concepts are applicable in real settings and that could contribute to the higher quality of software development processes and their products. 1
venue: Enase 2013 Proceedings of the 8th International Conference on Evaluation of Novel Approaches to Software Engineering
ta_rationale: Título sugere relevância potencial a processos ágeis de SW, mas sem abstract disponível não é possível confirmar se aborda process mining, modelagem estocástica ou forecasting de métricas de processo. Pode ser apenas framework de métodos ágeis genérico.
ft_rationale: O resumo descreve um framework de engenharia de métodos para melhorar a maturidade de processos de desenvolvimento ágil, mas não apresenta evidências de process mining (IC1), modelagem estocástica (IC2), forecasting de métricas (IC3) ou mineração de repositórios (IC4). Trata-se de proposta genérica de framework metodológico sem análise de dados de processos, logs de eventos ou previsões quantitativas.

title: Introduction to integration of the process mining to the knowledge framework for software processes
venue: Advances in Intelligent Systems and Computing
ta_rationale: Título sugere forte relevância a IC1 (integração de process mining em framework de processos de software), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação de escopo, aplicação prática e domínio. Necessária consulta ao texto completo para validar.
ft_rationale: PDF inacessível: não disponível em Unpaywall, Semantic Scholar nem OpenAlex. Capítulo de livro Springer atrás de paywall sem versão OA. Excluído por inacessibilidade de texto completo (sem PDF/OA disponível).

title: Jackson networks and Markov processes for resource allocation modeling
abstract: Measuring the productivities of engineers is one difficult task in software engineering. The popular technique used to evaluate the quality and productivity of engineers during software development is that supervisors and managers have to monitor engineers and keep track their work activities every day. Managers might use their common sense that can be unfair and incorrect to evaluate performance of engineers. Hence two new models encouraged fault repair model and discouraged fault arrival model that we propose solve these issues. A closed form derived from encouraged fault repair model is used to measure the productivities of engineers, and a closed form derived discouraged fault arrival model to evaluate the performance of testers. The limitation the fault repair rate [Luong 2001] is a constant is removed in the new model, engineering resource configuration model that is a Jackson network model with Markov chain configuration. Factors causing workforce available or unavailable are included into the model.
venue: Proceedings of the Annual Reliability and Maintainability Symposium
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (Jackson networks, Markov processes) potencialmente relevante a IC2, mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação se a aplicação é em processos de software ou em outro domínio (EC1). Venue (Reliability and Maintainability Symposium) não é tipicamente de SE/PM.
ft_rationale: O paper propõe modelos estocásticos (Jackson networks, Markov chains) para medir produtividade e desempenho de engenheiros, mas não aplica process mining nem estuda processos de desenvolvimento de software (fluxo, ciclo de vida, artefatos como commits/issues). A modelagem é teórica de alocação de recursos e repair/fault arrival, sem vinculação a dados reais de processos de SW ou avaliação em repositórios/event logs (EC3 — algoritmo sem aplicação concreta a processos de SW). Además, o foco é aval

title: Mining declarative models using time intervals
abstract: A common problem in process mining is the interpretation of the time stamp of events, e.g., whether it represents the moment of recording, or its occurrence. Often, this interpretation is left implicit. In this paper, we make this interpretation explicit using time intervals: an event occurs somewhere during a time window. The time window may be fine, e.g., a single point in time, or coarse, like a day. As each event is related to an activity within some process, we obtain for each activity a set of intervals in which the activity occurred. Based on these sets of intervals, we define ordering and simultaneousness relations. These relations form the basis of the discovery of a declarative process model describing the behavior in the event log.
venue: Ceur Workshop Proceedings
ta_rationale: Título sugere mining de modelos declarativos com intervalos de tempo, potencialmente relevante para process mining (IC1), mas ausência de resumo impede avaliação clara do domínio de aplicação (SW vs. BPM genérico) e se realmente aborda processos de desenvolvimento de software.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda process mining (descoberta de modelos declarativos) mas aplica-se a domínio genérico de BPM (Business Process Management), sem evidência de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software ou artefatos de SW (commits, issues, PR, CI/CD). Nenhum IC é atendido: IC1 requer aplicação a SW; IC2-IC4 também não se aplicam conforme descrito no resumo.

title: Non-homogeneous Markov software reliability model with imperfect repair
abstract: This paper reviews existing non-homogeneous Poisson process (NHPP) software reliability models and their limitations, and proposes a more powerful non-homogeneous Markov model for the fault detection/removal problem. In addition, this non-homogeneous Markov model allows for the possibility of a finite time to repair a fault and for imperfections in the repair process. The proposed scheme provides the basis for decision making both during the testing and the operational phase of the software product. Software behavior in the operational phase and the development test phase are related and the release time formulae are derived. Illustrations of the proposed model are provided.
venue: Proceedings IEEE International Computer Performance and Dependability Symposium IPDS
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica (Markov) em contexto de software reliability, potencialmente alinhado a IC2, mas sem resumo disponível é impossível confirmar se trata especificamente de processos de desenvolvimento de SW (nosso escopo) ou apenas de confiabilidade/dependabilidade de sistemas. A falta de abstract cria incerteza sobre a aplicação efetiva a processos de SW versus características de produto/sistema.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de modelagem estocástica (Markov) aplicada a software reliability e detecção/remoção de falhas durante testing e fase operacional. Embora use técnicas estocásticas (IC2 potencial), não há evidência de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software (commits, issues, CI/CD, event logs) nem a forecasting de métricas de processo (lead time, cycle time). O foco é em confiabilidade de sistema/produto e decisões de release, não em mineração ou análise de processos de desenvolvimento

title: Petri nets simulation for operational analytics based on process data: An overview

title: SBOAT: A stochastic BPMN analysis and optimisation tool
abstract: In this paper we present a description of a tool development framework, called SBOAT, for the quantitative analysis of graph based process modelling languages based upon the Business Process Modelling and Notation (BPMN) language, extended with intention preserving stochastic branching and parameterised reward annotations. SBOAT allows the optimisation of these processes by specifying optimisation goals by means of probabilistic control tree logic (PCTL). Optimisation is performed by means of an evolutionary algorithm where stochastic model checking, in the form of the PRISM model checker, is used to compute the fitness, the performance of a candidate in terms of the specified goals, of variants of a process. Our evolutionary algorithm approach uses a matrix representation of process models to efficiently allow mutation and crossover of a process model to be performed, allowing broad exploration of the space of possible models. We present a simple example of a distributed stochastic system where we determine a reachability property and the value of associated rewards in states of interest for a generated range of models. This example is taken from a case company in the Danish baking industry and will illustrate the practical applicability of this tool by helping the company analyse and optimise selected workflows.
venue: Opt I 2014 1st International Conference on Engineering and Applied Sciences Optimization Proceedings
ta_rationale: Título indica ferramenta de análise estocástica em BPMN, potencialmente relevante para IC2 (modelagem estocástica de processos). Porém, sem resumo disponível, não é possível confirmar se aplica-se a processos de software ou se é apenas BPM genérico (possível EC1 ou EC2). Necessária avaliação do texto completo.
ft_rationale: O paper apresenta uma ferramenta (SBOAT) para análise e otimização de processos BPMN com extensões estocásticas, aplicada a um caso na indústria de panificação dinamarquesa. Embora aborde modelagem estocástica (IC2 potencial), não há evidência de aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento de software — o domínio é BPM genérico em manufatura/indústria. title: Software Performance Analysis Using a Language Measure
abstract: This paper presents the software performance analysis using a finite state automation model. A signed real measure of formal languages has been used for quantitative evaluation of the software. This paper extends an earlier model based on a discrete time Markov chain to a semi-Markov process. As an example, a software rejuvenation policy is analyzed using the proposed approach.

Venue de controle (ACC 2003) é incomum para SE.

title: Software trustworthiness modeling based on Interactive Markov Chains
abstract: Research on trusted software is one of the branches of trusted computing. Software system is playing an increasingly important role in the information society. An error operation of software may result in economic damage and in the extreme cases cause deaths. When lives and fortunes depend on software, assurance of its quality becomes an issue of critical concern. At present, trusted degree is one of the key metrics for measuring the quality of software system. However, how to integrate the software trustworthiness into software design[7] and how to get the trusted degree as accurate as possible is still a problem in the field of trusted computing.
venue: Information Technology and Computer Application Engineering Proceedings of the 2013 International Conference on Information Technology and Computer Application Engineering Itcae 2013
ta_rationale: Título indica uso de Markov Chains (potencialmente IC2), mas ausência de resumo impede avaliação clara de (1) se modelagem se aplica a processos de SW (não apenas trustworthiness genérica de software), (2) se há event logs ou dados de processo envolvidos, (3) se não é exclusivamente teórico (EC3). Context vago: 'software trustworthiness' pode ser qualidade de código ou análise de comportamento de sistema, não necessariamente processo de desenvolvimento.

title: Statistical software testing with parallel modeling: A case study
abstract: Statistical software testing promises to offer a solution to the growing testing burden caused by the ever-increasing complexity of today's software systems. Nevertheless, the complexity of these systems makes it more difficult to provide a model to use as a basis for statistical testing. The flat and hierarchical modeling currently used to create operational profiles leads to enormous models when capturing the usage of these complex systems. This research describes a method to extend formal specification languages, intended for modeling complex systems, with statistical testing components. With this new procedure for defining operational profiles, we can generate statistical test cases from significantly larger models. This sets the stage for conducting future studies comparing statistical testing to other test strategies, such as structural testing.
venue: Proceedings International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering ISSRE
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estatística em testes de software, potencialmente relevante para IC2 (modelagem estocástica), mas sem abstract disponível é impossível confirmar se aborda processos de SW (vs. apenas testes como atividade isolada) ou se a modelagem é aplicada ao processo de desenvolvimento.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de modelagem estatística para testes de software (operational profiles, statistical testing), mas não aborda process mining, processos de desenvolvimento de software como fluxo, ou forecasting de métricas de processo. A 'modelagem estocástica' aqui é para gerar casos de teste, não para modelar ou prever o processo de desenvolvimento de SW (IC2 requer aplicação a processos de desenvolvimento, não apenas testes isolados). Nenhum IC é claramente atendido.

title: Stochastic model of human errors in software development: Impact of repair times
abstract: Most existing software reliability models estimate the failure intensity function during dynamic testing. While these models are useful engineering tools, they can not be applied to earlier life-cycle phases where pay-off is maximum in terms of avoiding later failures. Few models have been developed to capture phenomena occurring early in the life cycle and their impact on reliability and much research effort is still needed in this area. Our previous research efforts have led to the development of a stochastic model, which relates human errors committed during software development and debugging activities to the software failure intensity function. The software development schedule and other influencing factors (e.g., experience, schedule pressure, etc.) were used to predict human error rates. The model is based on several assumptions. In particular, it is assumed that repair is instantaneous. The present paper attempts to remove this assumption and assesses the impact of repair times on previous findings.
venue: Proceedings of the International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering ISSRE
ta_rationale: Título sugere modelagem estocástica aplicada a processos de software (IC2 potencial), mas ausência de resumo impede confirmação clara do escopo, metodologia e se aborda realmente processo de desenvolvimento de SW ou apenas confiabilidade de software como produto.
ft_rationale: O paper aborda modelagem estocástica (Markov/taxas de erro humano) aplicada ao processo de desenvolvimento de software, satisfazendo superficialmente IC2. Entretanto, o foco é em confiabilidade de software como produto (failure intensity function) e não em descoberta, conformance ou previsão de métricas de processo de desenvolvimento (lead time, cycle time, throughput). title: The development of a graphic multidimensional Markov Chain modeler to diagram large state spaces
abstract: Existing Markov modeling tools include high level Petri nets and process algebras, or low level statistical packages with programmed or form matrices. However, we and other modelers often describe our work in Markov Chain state space format. With the rising popularity of graphical tools, a graphical Markov Chain state space modeler makes sense to simplify development of these models. However, as networks become large and complex, the graphical tool must implement large state spaces quickly, by allowing the modeler to specify patterns for state and transition generation. Our graphic multidimensional Markov Chain modeler implements the Gauss-Seidel solution. It enables modelers to design state spaces graphically, eliminating programming and associated debugging. This tool can be used by programmers and non-programmers alike to speed up research efforts of all types, and can also be used as a training tool for probability or simulation courses teaching modeling. As an example application, we analyze a case study involving Common Radio Resource Management (CRRM) applied to load shared wireless networks.
venue: 42nd Annual Simulation Symposium 2009 Anss 2009 Part of the 2009 Spring Simulation Multiconference
ta_rationale: Título indica desenvolvimento de ferramenta para modelagem de Markov Chains com espaços de estado grandes, atendendo potencialmente IC2 (modelagem estocástica). Porém, sem abstract disponível, não é possível verificar se aplicação é em processos de software (requisito essencial) ou em domínio genérico de simulação, o que poderia caracterizar EC1 ou EC3.
ft_rationale: O resumo descreve uma ferramenta gráfica para modelagem de Markov Chains com aplicação em redes sem fio (CRRM), não em processos de desenvolvimento de software. Embora implemente modelagem estocástica (Markov), não há evidência de aplicação a artefatos de SW, processos de desenvolvimento ou event logs de software. Aplica-se EC1 (domínio fora de SW) e EC3 (algoritmo sem aplicação a processos de SW).

title: Time-based workflow mining
abstract: To improve mining accuracy of workflow models,a time-based algorithm was proposed.The execution time of tasks and the following wait time,succession relationships,intersection relationships between tasks were discovered by mining event logs.Setting up the edge matrix between tasks and the task parallel matrix,and finding relationships between edges,a workflow model with time factors was generated.An example was presented to demonstrate the improvements on model accuracy and the description ability of the proposed algorithm.

A venue (SEDE 2006) sugere contexto de engenharia de software, favorecendo inclusão após obtenção do abstract.

O exemplo mencionado não é detalhado o suficiente para validar aplicação em SE.

title: Towards automated conformance checking of ebBP-ST choreographies and corresponding WS-BPEL based orchestrations
abstract: Web Services technologies are a natural candidate for Business-to-Business integration (B2Bi). For crossorganizational processes, the concepts of “choreography” and “orchestration” are important. The term choreography denotes a model of a global view over message exchange scenarios, whereas the term orchestration focuses on models of the local implementation. While WS-BPEL is already kind of a de-facto standard in the field of orchestration languages, there does not exist a standard choreography language. We propose the usage of ebXML BPSS (ebBP) in order to provide choreography modeling at the business level. A frequent problem is to ensure and enforce the consistency and conformance of choreography and orchestration models which is often referred to as “conformance checking”. In this paper we examine a way to check the conformance between ebBP-ST (a subset of ebBP) choreographies and corresponding WS-BPEL based implementations. To achieve this check well-known and approved model checking methods and tools are used: First ebBP-ST choreographies are directly transformed into the process algebra CCS. Second, the low level WS-BPEL processes are analyzed for code blocks that implement choreography elements and the sequence of these code blocks is then mapped to CCS, too. Afterwards these formalized representations will be checked for bisimulation equivalence in order to reveal inconsistencies between the choreography and their implementations.
venue: Seke 2011 Proceedings of the 23rd International Conference on Software Engineering and Knowledge Engineering
ta_rationale: O título sugere conformance checking em orquestrações de processos de negócio (WS-BPEL), potencialmente relevante para IC1 (process mining em software), porém sem abstract disponível é impossível confirmar se aborda especificamente processos de desenvolvimento de software ou apenas BPM genérico. A falta de abstract impede avaliação segura contra EC1 e EC4.
ft_rationale: O paper trata de conformance checking entre choreografias ebBP-ST e orquestrações WS-BPEL em contexto de integração B2B genérica. Não aborda processos de desenvolvimento de software (IC1 requer artefatos de SW como commits, issues, PR, CI/CD), nem usa process mining para descobrir/analisar modelos, nem forecasting de métricas de processo, nem mineração de repositórios de SW. EC2 aplica-se: o foco é em tecnologias de Web Services e BPM genérico, não em processos de SE específicos.

title: Using Monte Carlo simulation for assessing software reliability
abstract: Abstract Simulation is presented here as a mathematical and computational technique use to analyze probabilistic models. Monte Carlo simulation, where the passage of time does not play a significant role, is presented here, with an emphasis on reliability applications. Monte Carlo simulation methods are often used when analytic methods become intractable, and they typically give a modeler added insight into the structure of a problem. As reliability and lifetime models become less mathematically tractable, Monte Carlo methods will have increasing importance. The topics addressed in here are basic variate generation techniques, the generation of order statistics, the generation of lifetimes for the accelerated life and proportional hazards lifetime models, and variate generation of a renewal or nonhomogeneous Poisson processes. The generation of order statistics is useful for simulating the lifetime of a series, parallel, or k ‐out‐of‐ n system. The generation of lifetime in the accelerated life and the Cox proportional hazards lifetime models is used to account for the impact of covariates on failure. The generation of a renewal or nonhomogeneous Poisson processes is used to model the failure times of a repairable system.
venue: Wmsci 2006 the 10th World Multi Conference on Systemics Cybernetics and Informatics Jointly with the 12th International Conference on Information Systems Analysis and Synthesis Isas 2006 Proc
ta_rationale: Título sugere uso de Monte Carlo (modelagem estocástica) em contexto de software, potencialmente alinhado com IC2. Porém, sem resumo disponível, é incerto se o foco é em processos de desenvolvimento de SW (escopo PATHCAST) ou apenas em confiabilidade/testing de software como produto (fora do escopo). title: Agent-based simulation of the software development process: a case study at AVL
authors: Bojan Spasic; Bhakti S. S. Onggo
abstract: Software development projects are difficult to manage due to the high uncertainty in their various phases. Simulation is one of the tools that has been used to help software project managers produce project plans. Research into software process simulation modeling (SPSM) shows the dominance of discrete-event simulation and system dynamics. This paper supports the use of ABS in SPSM. We propose a practical effort function to estimate developers' behavior. The other contribution of this paper is to demonstrate how the ABS model can be developed, calibrated and validated using data readily available to many software development companies/departments. This paper focuses on the construction phase of a tailored Rational Unified Process used in a geographically distributed software development department at AVL. The results look promising but more work needs to be done to include ABS into one of the mainstream simulation paradigms in SPSM.

title: Research and practice reciprocity in software process simulation
abstract: The young field of software process simulation was born out of research that stimulated imagination of many researchers who created a vision for addressing the software problem. Though vision has yet to be realized widely, need for field is growing and benefits are being seen slowly. Opportunity for continued growth of this field lies in reciprocity between research and industrial practice. An agenda for advancing software process simulation through this reciprocity is offered.

title: Software process simulation is simulation too: what can be learned from other domains of simulation?
abstract: Simulation is an important method and tool in many fields of engineering. Compared to these, simulation plays only a minor role in the field of software processes and software engineering. Examining this discrepancy, four theses are formulated as suggestions for future directions of software process simulation: 1. Simulation requires efforts, but “not simulating” might cause considerable costs as well, e.g. by wrong assumptions or expectations. These costs must be addressed and understood as well. 2. A model is always a simplification with many uncertainties. However, this is not a counter-argument by itself but must be evaluated in the perspective of purpose and available alternatives. 3. Future process simulation models must and will be much more complex than today. The necessary complexity can only be handled by relying on a rich set of mature components. This requires a joined effort and appreciation of the respective groundwork. 4. There are areas of software process modelling, which have already achieved some maturity, e.g. the interrelationships of volume of work, productivity, resources, and defect injection and removal. However, there are other aspects, which need further research to develop adequate modeling concepts, e.g. influence of architectural quality on later process stages, influence of process area capabilities within a dynamic simulation, or combined effects of human factors like time pressure, motivation, or knowledge acquisition.
